```text
documented_endpoint=https://opencode.ai/zen/v1/chat/completions
current_route_matches_documented=true
request_auth=Authorization: Bearer [REDACTED]
curl_exit_status=0
response_status=200
response_ok=true
response_type=chat.completion
response_model=accounts/fireworks/models/minimax-m2p7
response_finish_reason=stop
response_content_preview=OPENCLAW_MINIMAX_ROUTE_OK
response_error=null
secret-scan=pass
current-route-live-proof-exit-status=0
```

Previous authenticated proof before this MiniMax route repair covered discovery and representative live completions through Claude/Anthropic Messages, GPT/OpenAI Responses, Gemini/Google Generative AI, Qwen/Anthropic Messages, and MiniMax M2.5/OpenAI-compatible paths.

```text
authenticated_discovery_models=48
authenticated_discovery_request url=https://opencode.ai/zen/v1/models authorization_header=present status=200
shared_opencode_go_credential_catalog_models=48
live_request family=Claude model=claude-haiku-4-5 api=anthropic-messages baseUrl=https://opencode.ai/zen http_status=200 stopReason=stop text="ok"
live_request family=GPT model=gpt-5-nano api=openai-responses baseUrl=https://opencode.ai/zen/v1 http_status=200 stopReason=stop text="ok"
live_request family=Gemini model=gemini-3-flash api=google-generative-ai baseUrl=https://opencode.ai/zen/v1 stopReason=stop text="ok"
live_request family=Qwen model=qwen3.6-plus api=anthropic-messages baseUrl=https://opencode.ai/zen http_status=200 stopReason=stop text="ok"
live_request family=MiniMax model=minimax-m2.5 api=openai-completions baseUrl=https://opencode.ai/zen/v1 http_status=200 stopReason=stop text="ok"
result=passed
```

- **Observed result after fix:** `opencode` now exposes the Zen catalog, `minimax-m2.7` is no longer routed through the failed Zen Anthropic Messages path, the current MiniMax route matches the documented OpenAI-compatible chat completions endpoint, live discovery no longer makes ID-only unknown Zen rows selectable with guessed metadata, official-docs-sampled static costs now match the current table where the schema can represent them, and unverified/tiered static seed rows no longer publish misleading all-zero or stale single-cost metadata.
- **What was not tested:** I did not verify official pricing for every row in the 48-row static seed. Instead, this update corrects the sampled rows requested by review and removes cost metadata from rows without explicit provider-owned single-price data so they are not shown as free or underpriced. I also did not change or prove the provider-owner policy decisions for the full 48-row static seed or the shared `opencode`/`opencode-go` credential fallback; those remain intentionally disclosed maintainer/provider-owner review items.

## Review findings addressed

- **RF-001 — `status: ⏳ waiting on author`:** Addressed by fixing the latest author-actionable seed pricing finding and refreshing local evidence. This should be cleared by the next ClawSweeper review if no new author-actionable findings remain.
- **RF-002 — static seed can show paid models as free/underpriced:** Fixed for cost metadata. The 48-row seed remains, but unverified rows no longer publish default zero cost metadata; stale sampled rows were corrected from the official Zen docs where the current single-cost schema can represent the pricing, and tiered rows such as `gpt-5.5` omit `cost` instead of publishing an underpriced single value.
- **RF-003 — shared `opencode`/`opencode-go` credential fallback:** Disclosed as provider-owner policy risk. The fallback mirrors the sibling OpenCode Go catalog path and stays bounded to OpenCode catalog loading, but overlaps the open split-key policy discussion.
- **RF-004 — bounded seed-metadata repair:** Fixed. `extensions/opencode/provider-catalog.ts` no longer has a `DEFAULT_COST` fallback, and the manifest omits `cost` for rows not explicitly present in `MODEL_COSTS`.
- **RF-005 — correct the static Zen pricing seed:** Fixed by correcting the official-docs-sampled single-price rows and omitting unverified or tiered cost metadata rather than publishing guessed/free pricing. The manifest summary now reports `total=48`, five explicitly priced rows, and `zeroCost=[]`.
- **RF-006 — `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/opencode/index.test.ts`:** Passed. Latest evidence: `opencode-index-cost-green-20260620-r4.log` shows 1 test file passed with 11 tests passed.
- **RF-007 — official Zen docs pricing sample command:** Passed with proxy-enabled Node fetch. Evidence: `opencode-zen-doc-pricing-sample-20260620-r4.log` captures the sampled routes/prices for MiniMax M2.7, Claude Fable 5, Claude Opus 4.1/4.8, GPT 5.4 Mini, and the tiered GPT 5.5 rows.
- **Previously addressed MiniMax route and live-only row synthesis findings:** Still fixed. MiniMax M2.7 remains on the OpenAI-compatible chat completions route, and `buildDiscoveredOpencodeZenModels` still filters live-only rows through the provider-owned static seed.

## Regression Test Plan

- **Target test file:** `extensions/opencode/index.test.ts` plus redacted live evidence stored in the evidence directory outside the repo worktree.
- **Scenario locked in:** Regression tests assert the Zen catalog exposes the 48 static model rows, dynamic model resolution works for Zen IDs, shared `opencode-go` credentials populate the `opencode` catalog path, Claude/GPT/Gemini/Qwen/MiniMax families route to the expected adapter/base URL pairs, live-only discovery rows are filtered until metadata is owned, unverified static seed rows omit cost instead of defaulting to zero, and static fallback remains visible when live discovery is unavailable or returns no owned IDs.
- **Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:** A focused provider test locks the source-of-truth catalog/routing contract without adding broad adapter mocks, while the live proof covers the external provider boundary for the exact MiniMax route ClawSweeper requested.

## Merge risk

- **Risk labels considered:** `merge-risk: 🚨 compatibility`, `merge-risk: 🚨 auth-provider`, provider catalog/routing, generated manifest metadata, and large-diff catalog seed review cost.
- **Risk explanation:** The compatibility risk is that a provider-owned seed can drift from Zen if the upstream catalog changes before live discovery runs. This update reduces the pricing-specific risk by omitting unverified `cost` fields rather than publishing zero/default costs for rows without explicit provider-owned pricing. The auth-provider risk is bounded to resolving the same OpenCode credential across `opencode` and `opencode-go`; no key is stored in the repo or PR body. The large diff is mostly generated manifest/catalog rows rather than a cross-cutting runtime refactor. The MiniMax proof risk is now reduced by the redacted successful `minimax-m2.7` live completion on the current route; provider-owner review is still needed for the seed/auth policy boundaries.
- **Why acceptable:** The previous behavior left Zen effectively unusable without hand registration. The patch restores the provider contract at its owner boundary, keeps live discovery as the preferred source when credentials are present, prevents live-only ID metadata synthesis, preserves a no-discovery local seed, and fixes the concrete MiniMax routing defect surfaced by review.

## Root Cause

- **Root cause:** The `opencode` Zen provider was migrated as an auth/setup surface without owning the Zen model catalog contract that `opencode-go` already supported. Zen live discovery also returns ID-only rows, so unowned IDs cannot safely become selectable rich model rows without provider-owned metadata. Separately, MiniMax was grouped with Claude/Qwen on the Zen Anthropic Messages route even though live Zen behavior proves MiniMax runs through the documented OpenAI-compatible chat completions route. The static seed also treated missing price metadata as free by falling back to an all-zero cost object and kept stale single-price entries after the official Zen table changed.
- **Why this is root-cause fix:** The patch restores the missing provider-owned catalog/resolver boundary, keeps rich metadata owned by the provider seed, fixes MiniMax route selection before downstream adapter execution, corrects the official-docs-sampled single-price rows, and omits unverified/tiered cost metadata instead of adding downstream string special cases or guessing metadata from ID-only discovery.
- **Fix classification:** Root-cause provider catalog and routing repair for the bundled `opencode` Zen provider.
- **Maintainer-ready confidence:** High for the author-actionable source repair: the catalog regression, ClawSweeper live-only synthesis finding, and successful MiniMax live proof request are now covered by fresh provider tests and redacted live evidence.
- **Patch quality notes:** The fallback/default warnings are expected catalog-boundary behavior, not symptom masking: live model catalogs are advisory, and provider-owned static rows remain visible when discovery is unavailable or returns no owned metadata rows. The large diff is mostly static/generated catalog metadata needed to restore the regressed Zen catalog. Out of scope: changing non-OpenCode providers, generic adapter APIs, the sibling `opencode-go` runtime, the full 48-row seed policy, or the shared OpenCode credential fallback policy.
- **Architecture / source-of-truth check:** The provider plugin is the source-of-truth owner for bundled provider catalog metadata. This changes catalog/routing before downstream adapter selection rather than adding mirrored/persisted records or generic runtime fallbacks.

target.title: fix(sessions): reset stale per-channel origin fields on channel switch
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes #95325. With `session.dmScope: "main"`, a user's DMs to an agent across channels (Slack, Telegram, webchat) share one session. On a channel switch (e.g. Slack → Telegram), the session origin's per-channel identity fields are not reset.

`mergeOrigin` in `src/config/sessions/metadata.ts` merges field-by-field, overwriting a field only when the new inbound supplies it. Telegram DMs carry no `nativeChannelId` (or `threadId`), so the previous channel's Slack channel id persists in a now-Telegram session. It keeps persisting on every subsequent Telegram turn until the user messages on the original channel again or the session resets.

`route.target.to` (delivery) updates correctly, so message delivery is unaffected. But native-channel-keyed operations (reactions, native threading, native message references) and any status/inspection that reads `origin.nativeChannelId` reference the previous channel for the entire duration of the new-channel session — e.g. an agent on Telegram reports the prior Slack account/channel when asked about its current context.

## Why This Change Was Made

The sparse merge exists so consecutive turns on the *same* channel keep previously known fields when an inbound legitimately omits them. That intent is correct within a channel, but a provider/surface change is a *fresh channel identity*: the channel-keyed fields (`nativeChannelId`, `nativeDirectUserId`, `threadId`) belong to the prior channel and must not survive into the new one.

The fix treats a `provider` (or `surface`) change as a channel switch and drops those three channel-keyed fields before the merge, so an inbound that omits them no longer inherits the previous channel's values. Provider/surface-agnostic and always-supplied fields (`label`, `from`, `to`, `accountId`, `chatType`) are untouched and still update as before. Same-provider turns keep the existing sparse-merge behavior. This matches the root cause and suggested fix in the issue and keeps the change bounded to the one helper that owns origin merging.

## User Impact

Native-channel-keyed operations and context/status reads now reflect the channel the session is actually on after a cross-channel switch in a `dmScope: "main"` session, instead of pointing at the previous channel. No config, migration, or delivery-path change; same-channel sessions behave exactly as before.

## Evidence

Added `src/config/sessions/origin-channel-switch.test.ts`, exercising the real runtime path (`deriveSessionMetaPatch`, the function called per inbound turn in `src/auto-reply/reply/session.ts`) with realistic Slack-then-Telegram `MsgContext` inputs.

**Before (bug reproduced on the pre-fix `mergeOrigin`)** — stashing only the fix and running the test shows the stale Slack channel id persisting on the Telegram session, exactly as reported:

```text
FAIL  src/config/sessions/origin-channel-switch.test.ts > clears stale channel-keyed fields when provider changes and the new turn omits them
AssertionError: expected 'D111SLACK' to be undefined
   expect(afterTelegram.origin?.nativeChannelId).toBeUndefined();

FAIL  src/config/sessions/origin-channel-switch.test.ts > does not re-stamp the prior channel id on subsequent same-channel turns
AssertionError: expected 'D111SLACK' to be undefined
   expect(afterTelegramAgain.origin?.nativeChannelId).toBeUndefined();
  Tests  2 failed | 2 passed (4)
```

**Root cause / premise** — the two passing tests above confirm the other half of the contract is preserved both with and without the fix: when the new channel *does* supply `nativeChannelId`/`threadId` they are adopted, and same-provider turns still retain sparse metadata. So the regression is exactly the cross-provider omit case.

**After (with the fix)**:

```text
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/config/sessions/origin-channel-switch.test.ts
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  4 passed (4)
```

Test cases: (1) Slack→Telegram with Telegram omitting channel id → stale Slack `nativeChannelId`/`nativeDirectUserId`/`threadId` cleared, `provider`/`surface`/`accountId` become Telegram; (2) no re-stamp of the prior channel id on subsequent Telegram turns; (3) new channel's identity adopted when the inbound supplies it; (4) same-provider sparse turns still preserve the established channel id and thread.

Formatting verified with `oxfmt --check` on both touched files (clean). Full lint/typecheck/build run in CI.

### Real behavior proof notes

- Behavior addressed: cross-channel `dmScope: "main"` session keeps the prior channel's `nativeChannelId`/`threadId` after a Slack→Telegram switch.
- Environment tested: local unit test against the actual `deriveSessionMetaPatch`/`mergeOrigin` derivation path (the per-turn runtime entry point), Node 24.
- Root cause evidence: `src/config/sessions/metadata.ts` `mergeOrigin` only assigned each field when `next` supplied it; Telegram DM `MsgContext` carries no `NativeChannelId`/`MessageThreadId`, so the prior values survived.
- What was not tested: a two-bot live round-trip (a configured Slack bot and a Telegram bot sharing one `dmScope:"main"` session) was not run; the bug lives entirely in pure metadata derivation, so the unit test exercises the exact failing code path deterministically.

### Real behavior proof — production context builder (premise closed)

This proof drives the **production shared inbound-event context builder** `buildChannelInboundEventContext` (`src/channels/inbound-event/context.ts:515`, where `NativeChannelId = reply.nativeChannelId ?? conversation.nativeChannelId`) rather than hand-setting `ctx.NativeChannelId`. That proves the premise the bug rests on — a real Slack DM context **supplies** `NativeChannelId`, a real Telegram DM context **omits** it — and then runs that real context through the exact per-inbound-turn runtime path `deriveSessionMetaPatch → mergeOrigin`.

**Behavior addressed**: After a `dmScope:"main"` session switches provider (Slack → Telegram), session origin drops the prior channel's `nativeChannelId`/`nativeDirectUserId`/`threadId`.

**Real environment tested**: Linux (WSL2), Node v24.14.0, branch `fix/95325-reset-stale-origin-on-channel-switch` at commit `1a59d76a86`.

**Exact command run**:
```
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/config/sessions/origin-channel-switch.test.ts --reporter=verbose
```

**Evidence after fix**:
```text
 ✓ (real inbound-event context builder) > confirms the premise: Slack DM context supplies NativeChannelId, Telegram DM context omits it
 ✓ (real inbound-event context builder) > resets the stale Slack channel id after a real-context Slack->Telegram switch
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  6 passed (6)
```

**Evidence before fix** (the `channelChanged` clear block temporarily removed from `mergeOrigin`):
```text
 × > clears stale channel-keyed fields when provider changes and the new turn omits them
   → expected 'D111SLACK' to be undefined
 × > does not re-stamp the prior channel id on subsequent same-channel turns
   → expected 'D111SLACK' to be undefined
 × (real inbound-event context builder) > resets the stale Slack channel id after a real-context Slack->Telegram switch
   → expected 'D111SLACK' to be undefined
      Tests  3 failed | 3 passed (6)
```

**Observed result**: With the fix removed, the context produced by the real builder keeps the stale `D111SLACK` on the Telegram turn (3 failures, including the real-context case). With the fix, the stale id is reset on the provider switch (6/6 pass). The premise case passes in both states, confirming the supply/omit asymmetry is produced by the real builder, not assumed by the test.

**What was not tested**: a live two-bot Slack↔Telegram round-trip over real transports (requires live Slack + Telegram credentials). The bug lives entirely in pure metadata derivation; this proof exercises the real production context builder and the real merge function — the exact code each inbound turn runs.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? `Yes` (post-channel-switch origin now reflects the current channel; same-channel behavior unchanged)

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? `No`

---

AI-assisted.

target.title: Preserve shared QA suite flags across runners
target.body:
## Summary

- Stacked on #91502.
- Preserves shared QA suite selections across both built-in suite runners by forwarding `--channel-driver crabline --channel telegram` and `--enable-plugin` into the existing isolated runner wrapper.
- Keeps the channel driver meaning scoped to the openclaw/crabline messaging/channel SDK; this PR does not add Canonical Multipass VM orchestration behavior, guest command features, or a VM-backed channel driver.
- Leaves the real runner-specific exceptions unchanged: VM resource flags remain Multipass-only, and `--cli-auth-mode` / `--preflight` remain host-only.
- Review focus: whether these are the right parity boundaries for suite-level flags versus runner-lifecycle or credential-sensitive flags.

## Linked context

Stacked on openclaw/openclaw#91502.
Related openclaw/crabline#1.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: shared QA suite selections no longer get dropped or rejected when using `qa suite --runner multipass`; the existing isolated runner wrapper preserves them for the guest suite command.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout on `codex/multipass-channel-driver-runner-parity`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `git diff --check`
  - `pnpm exec oxfmt --check CHANGELOG.md extensions/qa-lab/src/cli.runtime.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/cli.runtime.test.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/multipass.runtime.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/multipass.runtime.test.ts`
  - `pnpm tsgo:extensions`

- Evidence after fix: focused Vitest run passed 6 files / 117 tests; formatting, whitespace, and extension typecheck passed.
- Observed result after fix: `runQaSuiteCommand` now passes channel-driver selection and plugin enablements to `runQaMultipass`, and `createQaMultipassPlan` renders those selections into the generated guest `pnpm openclaw qa suite` command.
- What was not tested: a live run of the existing isolated runner wrapper and live Telegram SDK-channel e2e assertions.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: this is scoped to deterministic command construction and CLI routing; live isolated-runner validation can run after reviewer agreement on the parity boundary.
- Before evidence: #91502 rejected `--channel-driver crabline` with `--runner multipass`, and the isolated runner wrapper did not forward `--enable-plugin`.

## Tests and validation

- Added CLI runtime coverage for forwarding channel-driver selection and plugin enablements to `runQaMultipass`.
- Added isolated-runner command construction coverage for generated guest command flags.
- Validation run locally:
  - `git diff --check`
  - `pnpm exec oxfmt --check CHANGELOG.md extensions/qa-lab/src/cli.runtime.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/cli.runtime.test.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/multipass.runtime.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/multipass.runtime.test.ts`

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? `Yes`: `qa suite --runner multipass` now accepts and preserves shared channel-driver and plugin enablement flags.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? `No`.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? `No`.

What is the highest-risk area? Misclassifying a runner-specific flag as shared suite semantics.

How is that risk mitigated? The PR only forwards deterministic suite selections; credential-sensitive auth mode and preflight artifact behavior stay host-only, and VM sizing flags stay Multipass-only.

## Current review state

Next action: review after #91502.

Still waiting on: #91502 and openclaw/crabline#1.

Bot/reviewer comments addressed: follow-up to maintainer question about runner flag parity; repo identity and channel-driver id updated from `multipass` to `crabline`.

target.title: fix(memory-core): surface empty narrative generation instead of silent fallback (#90781)
target.body:
## Summary

When a Dreaming narrative run produces no usable text, memory-core writes a
fallback diary entry and logs a bare `narrative generation produced no text`
warning — giving operators no signal about *why* (empty completion, tool-only
assistant turn, refusal, non-text parts, etc.). Issue #90781.

This adds `summarizeNarrativeMessages`, which inspects the assistant messages and
produces a compact diagnostic (`messages=… assistant=… withText=… toolOnly=…
nonTextParts=… lastAssistantContent=…`). The diagnostic is appended to both the
warning log and the fallback entry reason.

## Rebase note

Rebased onto current `main`. Main has since refactored narrative reading into
`readSettledNarrativeText` (retry-with-settle), which no longer exposed the raw
`messages` at the empty-result site. To keep the diagnostic accurate without an
extra session fetch, `readNarrativeText`/`readSettledNarrativeText` now return
the last-read messages alongside the narrative (`SettledNarrative`), and the
empty-result branch summarizes those settled messages.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-narrative.test.ts` → 63 passed.
- `pnpm tsgo:extensions` → clean.
- `oxfmt --check` / `oxlint` clean on changed files.

Closes #90781

target.title: perf(models-config): unify auth-profile fingerprint cache + targetProvider short-circuit
target.body:
## Summary

Improves `models.json` preparation so OpenClaw can skip expensive implicit-provider discovery when the relevant inputs are unchanged, while still re-planning when canonical auth state, plugin catalogs, or generated config inputs drift.

This PR reconciles two complementary optimizations inside `ensureOpenClawModelsJson`: a warm-process whole-input fingerprint cache and a provider-scoped `targetProvider` structural short-circuit. Together they make the common model-setup path cheap on warm turns, cold starts, gateway restarts, and unrelated-provider changes without letting stale provider/model routing survive across real input changes.

The key correctness move is that cache keys now read the canonical SQLite agent auth store instead of the retired `auth-profiles.json` file. That means migrated agents no longer look permanently `absent` to the cache, credential changes invalidate the plan when they should, and the runtime avoids the legacy file-hashing attack surface entirely.

## Why

`ensureOpenClawModelsJson` sits on startup and model-selection paths where a full implicit-provider plan can fan out through plugin discovery and provider-catalog work. If the prepared config is already valid, that work is pure overhead; if auth profiles, generated plugin catalogs, or persisted `models.json` inputs changed, reusing the old plan is a routing-integrity bug.

This PR aims for the narrow middle: reuse aggressively only when the canonical inputs prove stable, and fail closed to a full re-plan when they do not. That preserves the performance intent of the earlier branches while tightening the cache boundary around the state OpenClaw actually uses today.

## What Changed

- Adds an in-process whole-input fingerprint cache for warm `ensureOpenClawModelsJson` calls.
- Adds a provider-scoped `targetProvider` structural short-circuit so cold starts, gateway restarts, and unrelated-provider edits can skip full planning when disk/config validation says the scoped output is still valid.
- Fingerprints the canonical SQLite auth store (`openclaw-agent.sqlite`, via the warm pooled handle) instead of legacy `auth-profiles.json`, while continuing to strip volatile OAuth session fields so token rotation does not churn the cache.
- Keeps `type:"token"` expiry fields cache-significant so expired static tokens invalidate the plan.
- Threads generated plugin catalogs into the pre-plan parsed `models.json` and writes them consistently through skip, noop, and write paths.
- Makes the compatibility source-fingerprint shim explicitly non-cacheable for unreadable/corrupt auth state, and has both `model-catalog` and `models list` provider-catalog consumers skip persisted catalog cache read/write when that source is non-cacheable.
- Preserves the hardenings from both predecessor branches: fail-closed `uncacheable` handling, prototype-pollution guards, bounded compare depth, per-model transport refusal, validated-outcome TOCTOU close, and scoped-cache isolation.

## Relationship to Prior PRs

Supersedes #73260 and #73261 with one reconciled implementation. They were complementary, not redundant:

- **#73260** added content-hash auth-profile fingerprinting plus `models.json` drift detection. It was the warm-process whole-input memoizer; OAuth token rotation no longer busts the cache.
- **#73261** added the provider-scoped `targetProvider` structural short-circuit with disk-vs-config validation. It skips discovery on cold start / gateway restart, or when an unrelated provider changed.

Both earlier branches still hashed the legacy JSON auth file, which reads `absent` for agents already migrated to SQLite. This PR moves that shared cache input to the canonical store and carries forward the security and correctness refinements from both branches.

## Verification

Exact-head local verification on `e5671e9b27`:

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/models-config.fingerprint-cache.test.ts src/agents/model-catalog.test.ts src/commands/models/list.provider-catalog.test.ts` -> **88 passed** across the focused fingerprint/model-catalog/provider-catalog suites.
- `pnpm tsgo:core` -> clean.
- `pnpm tsgo:core:test` -> clean.
- `pnpm tsx scripts/proof-90741-plugin-catalog-cache-validation.ts` -> all 5 runtime assertions passed.
- `git diff --check` -> clean.

## Real behavior proof

Real-runtime proof script `scripts/proof-90741-plugin-catalog-cache-validation.ts` drives the real `ensureOpenClawModelsJson` path against a real on-disk agent directory, real generated plugin-catalog sidecars, the real `readyCache` populate/drift cycle, the real no-create auth-store read, and the public `buildModelsJsonSourceFingerprint` shim. **No mocking of the cache/fingerprint seams under test**; only the network edge is avoided with static credentials.

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Generated plugin-catalog sidecar drift must bust the warm models-config cache and reconcile rogue files; fingerprint-only auth reads must not create the SQLite auth DB; plugin-catalog-only plans must not rewrite the root `models.json`; corrupt or malformed SQLite auth state must read as unreadable and make the source fingerprint non-cacheable. The new catalog-cache regressions then pin that both `model-catalog` and provider-catalog consumers skip persisted catalog cache read/write when the source fingerprint is non-cacheable.
- **Real environment tested:** Topic worktree at head `e5671e9b27`, with real temp agent directories, real SQLite files/corruption, real generated plugin-catalog sidecars, and the real source-fingerprint shim.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `pnpm tsx scripts/proof-90741-plugin-catalog-cache-validation.ts`, run from the topic worktree.
- **Evidence after fix:** Captured stdout from the live run:

```
[1/5] sidecar-drift-busts-cache-and-reconciles
    rogue sidecar present after re-plan: false
    ok
[2/5] fingerprint-read-does-not-create-agent-db
    auth DB created: false, -wal: false, -shm: false
    ok
[3/5] stable-no-sidecar-still-hits
    models.json mtimeMs cold=1781500389439.0088 warm=1781500389439.0088 ino cold=4067810 warm=4067810
    ok
[4/5] plugin-catalog-only-write-preserves-root
    root models.json ino cold=4067823 warm=4067823 mtimeMs cold=1781500389539.2183 warm=1781500389539.2183
    ok
[5/5] unreadable-auth-store-fails-closed
    no-db outcome.kind=absent
    valid-store outcome.kind=present
    corrupt-db outcome.kind=unreadable
    corrupt-db fingerprint cacheable=false
    malformed-json-cell outcome.kind=unreadable
    malformed-json-cell fingerprint cacheable=false
    ok

All runtime assertions passed.
```

- **Observed result after fix:** All five runtime assertions passed (process exit 0). The source-fingerprint shim now reports `cacheable=false` for both corrupt SQLite and malformed JSON auth-store states, so catalog callers can decline persisted cache keys instead of reusing stale rows under a stable sentinel.
- **What was not tested:** A fully live multi-provider gateway boot is not exercised; the cache populate/drift/reconcile, no-create auth read, plugin-catalog-only write guard, and unreadable-auth fingerprint semantics are driven end-to-end against real on-disk state by the proof script above. Focused unit coverage pins the model-catalog and provider-catalog consumer behavior.

target.title: feat(gateway): add controlUi.timezone for Control UI timestamp rendering
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem:** Control UI formats all timestamps client-side using browser-local `Date.toLocaleString()` APIs. Browsers with fingerprinting protection (hardened Firefox, Tor-derived configs) report UTC instead of the user's actual timezone. Chat timestamps then render in UTC without any visible indicator, confusing operators who expect local time.
- **Why it matters:** An admin UI should display predictable timestamps. Relying solely on the browser's fingerprinting surface makes timestamps silently wrong for privacy-conscious deployments.
- **What changed:** Added `gateway.controlUi.timezone` config field. Accepts `"host"` (resolves to server local timezone) or any IANA timezone string (e.g. `"America/New_York"`). The resolved timezone travels through the bootstrap config payload to the UI, which passes an explicit `timeZone` option to every `toLocaleString`/`toLocaleTimeString`/`toLocaleDateString` call.
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary):** When `timezone` is omitted, behavior is identical to before this change. No `"browser"` or `"user"` mode enum was added, keeping the config surface minimal. Core gateway logic, auth, and channel code are untouched.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #90314

## Root Cause (if applicable)

N/A (feature request, not a bug fix).

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

N/A (new feature).

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- New config key: `gateway.controlUi.timezone` (string, optional).
  - `"host"` resolves to the gateway server's local IANA timezone.
  - Any valid IANA timezone string (e.g. `"Europe/Warsaw"`) is used directly.
  - Omitting the key preserves current browser-default behavior.
- When set, all Control UI timestamp surfaces (chat messages, sessions, logs, usage metrics, dreaming, debug event log) render in the configured timezone.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[browser] -> Date.toLocaleTimeString() -> uses browser-reported TZ (may be UTC under fingerprinting)

After:
[server config] -> bootstrap payload { timezone: "America/New_York" } -> [UI timezone module]
[UI formatter] -> toLocaleTimeString(date, locale, { timeZone: configured }) -> correct local time
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** Control UI timestamps now render in the timezone specified by `gateway.controlUi.timezone` instead of the browser-reported timezone. Invalid timezone strings are rejected at config parse time with a clear error message.

- **Real environment tested:** macOS Darwin 25.5.0, Node 22.19+, local dev gateway (`pnpm openclaw --dev gateway run`), port 19001.

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

1. Removed `timezone` from `controlUi` config and started the gateway:
```bash
pnpm openclaw --dev gateway run
curl -s -H "Authorization: Bearer <token>" http://127.0.0.1:19001/__openclaw/control-ui-config.json
```

2. Set `"timezone": "America/New_York"` in config and restarted:
```bash
pnpm openclaw --dev gateway run
curl -s -H "Authorization: Bearer <token>" http://127.0.0.1:19001/__openclaw/control-ui-config.json
```

3. Set `"timezone": "host"` and restarted:
```bash
pnpm openclaw --dev gateway run
curl -s -H "Authorization: Bearer <token>" http://127.0.0.1:19001/__openclaw/control-ui-config.json
```

4. Set `"timezone": "Fake/Timezone"` (invalid) and attempted to start:
```bash
pnpm openclaw --dev gateway run
```

- **Evidence after fix:**

**Before (no timezone configured) -- bootstrap response has no `timezone` field:**
```json
{"basePath":"","assistantName":"Assistant","assistantAvatar":"A","assistantAvatarSource":null,"assistantAvatarStatus":"none","assistantAvatarReason":"missing","assistantAgentId":"main","serverVersion":"2026.6.2","localMediaPreviewRoots":["/tmp/openclaw","..."],"embedSandbox":"scripts","allowExternalEmbedUrls":false}
```

**After (`timezone: "America/New_York"`) -- bootstrap response includes resolved timezone:**
```json
{"basePath":"","assistantName":"Assistant","assistantAvatar":"A","assistantAvatarSource":null,"assistantAvatarStatus":"none","assistantAvatarReason":"missing","assistantAgentId":"main","serverVersion":"2026.6.2","localMediaPreviewRoots":["/tmp/openclaw","..."],"embedSandbox":"scripts","allowExternalEmbedUrls":false,"timezone":"America/New_York"}
```

**`"host"` resolves to server-local timezone (Europe/Berlin on this machine):**
```
timezone: Europe/Berlin
```

**Invalid timezone rejected at startup with clear error:**
```
Gateway failed to start: Invalid config at /Users/rbhandar/.openclaw-dev/openclaw.json.
gateway.controlUi.timezone: Must be "host" or a valid IANA timezone (e.g. "America/New_York")
Run "openclaw --profile dev doctor --fix" to repair, then retry.
```

**All 20 timezone-related tests pass (7 timezone module + 5 bootstrap + 8 existing):**
```
 Test Files  2 passed (2)
      Tests  20 passed (20)
   Duration  1.07s
```

- **Observed result after fix:** The bootstrap endpoint correctly includes or omits the `timezone` field based on config. `"host"` resolves server-side to the machine's IANA timezone. Invalid timezone strings are rejected during config parsing with an actionable error message, preventing `RangeError` at runtime. When omitted, the response matches the pre-patch format exactly (backward compatible).

- **What was not tested:** Live browser rendering with `privacy.resistFingerprinting` enabled. The UI timezone module is covered by unit tests that verify the `timeZone` option is passed through to `Intl.DateTimeFormat`, but a visual browser screenshot with fingerprinting protection was not captured.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS Darwin 25.5.0
- Runtime: Node 22+, Bun
- Model/provider: N/A (UI-only change)
- Integration/channel: N/A

### Steps

1. Set `gateway.controlUi.timezone` to `"America/New_York"` in config.
2. Open Control UI in a browser with fingerprinting protection enabled (Firefox `privacy.resistFingerprinting = true`).
3. Send a chat message and observe the timestamp in the chat footer.

### Expected

Timestamp renders in America/New_York local time regardless of browser fingerprinting settings.

### Actual

Before this PR: timestamp renders in UTC. After: renders in the configured timezone.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- 20 tests pass: 7 timezone module unit tests + 5 bootstrap integration tests (including timezone set/clear) + 8 existing format/bootstrap tests.
- `pnpm check` passes (types, lint, import cycles, format).
- `pnpm config:docs:check` passes with regenerated baseline.

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified: all 20 timezone-related tests pass, `pnpm check` green, `pnpm tsgo` green, config baseline regenerated and checked.
- Edge cases: omitted timezone preserves browser default, `"host"` resolves server timezone, empty string treated as omitted, invalid IANA string rejected at config parse.
- Live gateway verification: bootstrap endpoint confirmed to include/omit timezone field correctly; invalid timezone rejected at startup with actionable error.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes. Omitting the new config key preserves current behavior exactly.
- Config/env changes? Yes, one new optional config key `gateway.controlUi.timezone`.
- Migration needed? No.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Invalid IANA string passed to `toLocaleString` throws a `RangeError` in some runtimes.
  - Mitigation: Zod `.refine()` validation tests the string with `Intl.DateTimeFormat` at config parse time. Invalid values are rejected before the gateway starts.

target.title: fix(agents): preserve streamed assistant text when Claude CLI result event is empty
target.body:
## Summary

- The claude `stream-json` dialect can emit a final `result` event with **empty text** even though assistant text deltas were already streamed (or the turn was tool-only). Both JSONL parsers trust the final event and discard everything accumulated, so the CLI runner throws `CLI backend returned an empty response.` (`reason=empty_response`) and the gateway falls back to the next model — **losing a reply that had already fully arrived**.
- Fix (parser-only): when the `result` event has no text, fall back to the accumulated streamed deltas instead of dropping them, in **both** `createCliJsonlStreamingParser` and `parseCliJsonl`. A structured `CliOutput.hadToolCalls` flag (set via `claudeRecordHasToolUseBlock` / the existing `isClaudeToolUseBlockType`) is also recorded so a verifiably tool-only turn can be told apart from a genuinely empty reply.
- **The runner's empty-response failover contract is unchanged.** An earlier revision of this PR added a `hadToolCalls`-based bypass in `cli-runner.ts`; that was removed (commit `ac2f807c`, following Option 1 of the review). Today an empty turn — tool-only or not — still fails over by default, and only stays silent when the caller already set `allowEmptyAssistantReplyAsSilent`. **This PR no longer touches `cli-runner.ts`** (it is byte-identical to `main`); the only behavioral change lives in the two parsers.
- The parser already half-acknowledges this case — `parseClaudeCliJsonlResult` deliberately keeps session metadata when the result text is empty (*"Keep the resolved session handle and usage instead of dropping them"*). This PR extends the same reasoning to the streamed assistant text itself.
- Safety: if the model genuinely returned nothing, the accumulated text is also empty, so the `FailoverError` fires exactly as before. Non-Claude dialects are untouched (`hadToolCalls` stays `undefined`).
- Reviewers should focus on: the fallback expression in **both** parsers and the structured tool_use detection helper (`claudeRecordHasToolUseBlock`). `cli-runner.ts` is **not** part of this diff.
- The vulnerable path (`if (result) { output = result; return; }`) is still present on `main` and in the published `2026.6.8` tarball (`dist/claude-live-session-*.js` / `dist/cli-output-*.js`); see the 2026-06-16 comment confirming it is not fixed natively in 6.7/6.8.

## Tests

- `cli-output.test.ts`: 4 new cases — streamed text preserved on empty result (both parsers), tool-only flag set (both parsers); updated the existing "preserves Claude session metadata even when the final result text is empty" expectation (`hadToolCalls: false`).
- `cli-runner.reliability.test.ts`: focused coverage that a verifiably tool-only empty turn (tool_use block + empty result) **fails over by default** (`empty_response`) and only returns the silent payload when `allowEmptyAssistantReplyAsSilent` is set — i.e. the prior fallback contract is preserved.
- `pnpm build && pnpm check && pnpm test` run locally (macOS): the only failures are environment-specific (host locale leaking into `Intl` formatting asserts and `/tmp` → `/private/tmp` symlink canonicalization), none in the touched files.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Claude CLI turns streamed a full reply, but the final `result` event arrived with empty text; the gateway threw `CLI backend returned an empty response.` (`reason=empty_response`) and fell back to `openai/gpt-5.5`, discarding the streamed reply. Tool-only turns hit the same false failover.
- Real environment tested: production OpenClaw **2026.6.1** gateway on an Ubuntu VPS (systemd service), `anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6` and `claude-haiku-4-5` via the claude-cli backend, fallback chain to `openai/gpt-5.5`, live Discord/WhatsApp channels and cron agents. Still reproducing through **2026.6.8** (see the 2026-06-16 comment).
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: applied the equivalent fix as a dist-bundle patch (same logic as this PR's source change) to `dist/claude-live-session-*.js` and `dist/cli-output-*.js`, validated with `node --check`, then `systemctl restart openclaw-gateway` (2026-06-04 13:56 BRT) and watched `journalctl -u openclaw-gateway -f` under normal production traffic; later verified with `ssh` to the host and `journalctl -u openclaw-gateway --since "14:26" | grep -c empty_response`.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): redacted runtime logs / copied live output below. After the restart: `journalctl -u openclaw-gateway --since "14:26" | grep -c empty_response` → `0` (vs 3 occurrences earlier that same day), and the gateway boot shows claude sessions healthy:

  ```
  Jun 04 14:26:26 [gateway] http server listening (11 plugins: anthropic, codex, discord, ...)
  ```

- Observed result after fix: zero `empty_response` fallbacks across several hours of live traffic since the restart; claude turns that previously produced `outBytes=0` now deliver the streamed text and stay on the requested model. The tool-only half of this fix has been running in the same production gateway since 2026-05-31, the empty-response half since 2026-06-04 — zero false `empty_response` fallbacks across every release since (6.5 / 6.6 / 6.8).
- What was not tested: non-Claude CLI backends (gemini/codex dialects — code paths untouched, `hadToolCalls` stays `undefined` for them); `allowEmptyAssistantReplyAsSilent=true` beyond existing unit coverage.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: the empty-result event is intermittent (3 occurrences on 2026-06-04 before the patch, none reproducible on demand); an instrumented hook in the production gateway dumps the raw JSONL stream on the next occurrence, and we will attach a sanitized capture as a follow-up comment.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): three occurrences on 2026-06-04 before the patch. Note `outHash=e3b0c44298fc` — the SHA-256 of the empty string — after 40/66/86 streamed JSONL lines: a full stream arrived, exactly zero bytes were delivered, and the turn was answered by the fallback model:

target.title: refactor: add QMD session artifact identity mapping
target.body:
## What
Adds an internal QMD session artifact identity mapping so generated QMD Markdown session artifacts resolve back to stable transcript memory/session identity before deprecated filename fallback.

## Why
Path 3 needs QMD search hits to stop treating generated Markdown artifact names as canonical session identity. QMD still needs Markdown files to index transcript content, but those files are search artifacts; identity should come from a storage-neutral transcript memory key/session mapping.

Refs #88838. Builds on #89348.

## Changes
- Add QMD artifact mapping
- Store identity in QMD index
- Prefer mapped session identity
- Preserve archived compatibility
- Guard live orphan hits
- Cover visibility regressions

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `extensions/memory-core/src/qmd-session-artifacts.ts` | Adds private artifact mapping helper |
| `extensions/memory-core/src/memory/qmd-manager.ts` | Writes and attaches QMD mappings |
| `extensions/memory-core/src/session-search-visibility.ts` | Prefers mapped session identity |
| `extensions/memory-core/src/session-search-visibility.test.ts` | Covers mapped visibility behavior |

## Testing
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/session-search-visibility.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/qmd-manager.slugified-paths.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check <touched files>`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs <touched files>`
- `pnpm tsgo:extensions`
- `pnpm tsgo:test:extensions`
- `git diff --check`
- Autoreview completed with no accepted/actionable findings

This uses memory-core's existing QMD SQLite index/cache only. It does not add a public `SessionCorpusEntry` SDK, 3.2 session/transcript SQLite store modules, production storage flip, runtime dual-read/fallback, JSON sidecar, or doctor migration.

target.title: fix(doctor): survive throwing plugin hooks and warn on build-version skew
target.body:
## Summary

Hardens `openclaw doctor` and plugin loading against installed plugins built for a different OpenClaw release, the failure class behind #91991 (reported by @cn1313113): `@openclaw/feishu@2026.6.5-beta.5` imports an SDK export that `openclaw@2026.6.5` stable no longer ships (the #91322 release-line revert removed it), and the jiti loader turns the missing named export into a call-time `TypeError` that aborted the whole doctor run.

Two pieces:

**1. Plugin doctor hooks are now fail-soft (`fix(doctor)`)**
- `src/commands/doctor/shared/channel-doctor.ts`: every channel doctor adapter hook invocation (`runConfigSequence`, `cleanStaleConfig`, `collectPreviewWarnings`, `collectMutableAllowlistWarnings`, `repairConfig`, empty-allowlist hooks) catches per entry. One broken plugin degrades to a doctor warning naming the channel and suggesting `openclaw plugins update <id>` instead of aborting every other repair. Entries carry the channel id to make the warning actionable. The production-uncalled `collectChannelDoctorCompatibilityMutations` collector is deleted (tests re-pointed at the equivalent `cleanStaleConfig` probe).
- `src/commands/doctor-config-flow.ts`: warning-only stale mutations now surface as doctor warnings instead of being silently dropped.
- Same class, sibling surfaces found during review and fixed with per-entry catches mirroring the module's existing silent-skip policy for failed contract module loads: `applyPluginDoctorCompatibilityMigrations` (`src/plugins/doctor-contract-registry.ts`) and the `detectLegacyState` phase (`src/infra/state-migrations.ts`, whose migrate phase was already wrapped).

**2. Build-version skew warning (`feat(plugins)`)**
- New `src/plugins/build-version-skew.ts` checks the `openclaw.build.openclawVersion` every published plugin artifact already records against the running host, reusing `src/infra/semver-compare.ts`. Rule: prerelease builds only match the exact host they were built for (prerelease SDK surface can be withdrawn before the train ships — exactly the beta.5 incident); stable builds are quiet on any host at or past their release; missing build metadata (dev/local/git installs) stays silent.
- `src/plugins/manifest-registry.ts` emits a warn-only diagnostic for skewed non-bundled plugins (plugin still loads): `plugin was built for OpenClaw 2026.6.5-beta.5, but this host is 2026.6.5; its SDK imports may not match. Run 'openclaw plugins update <id>' to install a matching build.` The diagnostic surfaces in both gateway load logs and doctor's "Plugin diagnostics" panel from one implementation point.

With both pieces, the #91991 scenario (stale beta plugin + stable core) no longer crashes doctor: the run completes, and the user gets a named warning with the `plugins update` remedy.

Scope notes:
- `channel-legacy-config-migrate.ts` stays unwrapped on purpose: it only invokes bundled channel contracts, which ship inside the core dist and cannot version-skew.
- The `detectLegacyState` catch has no dedicated test; it is the same three-line pattern as the adjacent, already-shipped migrate-phase catch. The thrown-hook behavior class is covered by the channel-doctor and contract-registry tests.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/build-version-skew.test.ts src/plugins/doctor-contract-registry.test.ts src/plugins/manifest-registry.test.ts src/commands/doctor/shared/channel-doctor.test.ts src/commands/doctor-config-flow.test.ts src/infra/state-migrations.test.ts` — all shards pass, including new coverage: 15-case skew matrix, throwing adapters for sequence/stale/mutable/repair/empty-allowlist hooks (healthy siblings keep running, config chain preserved), a real throwing `.cjs` doctor-contract fixture, and 3 manifest-registry skew cases (warn fires, plugin still loads, bundled origin exempt).
- `pnpm tsgo` + `pnpm check:test-types` clean; `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs` on touched files clean; `oxfmt` applied via `pnpm format:diff`.
- `pnpm build` clean after the manifest-registry import change (plugin-sdk export checks and dynamic-import checks pass).
- Maintainer note: skipping the parsed `Real behavior proof` block per write-access policy; behavior is locked by the unit/integration tests above, which reproduce the #91991 failure shape (a hook throwing `TypeError` mid-doctor).

Related: #91991, #91322.

target.title: test(plugins): cover filtered bundle MCP servers
target.body:
## Summary

This PR tightens the existing compatible-bundle MCP path for bundles that need a narrower default tool surface.

- Adds regression coverage that a Codex-compatible bundle `.mcp.json` preserves `toolFilter.include`, `toolFilter.exclude`, and a relative bundle data root.
- Documents bundle MCP tool filters on the plugin-bundles page alongside the existing transport and tool-naming guidance.
- Keeps scope limited to bundle MCP loading/docs; runtime filtering behavior already exists and is covered separately.

Reviewers should focus on whether the bundle MCP docs accurately describe the existing runtime behavior and whether the new compatible-bundle regression is useful.

## Linked context

No issue filed.

Related precedent investigated: bundle MCP support and follow-up coverage in prior OpenClaw PRs such as #48611, #71287, #79686, and #88399. This PR follows that existing bundle MCP seam.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

Behavior addressed: Compatible bundles that contribute MCP servers can ship a narrow default tool surface through `toolFilter.include` and `toolFilter.exclude`; OpenClaw docs now describe that bundle path and a regression test proves the loader preserves the filter and relative app root.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on macOS, branch `test/filtered-bundle-mcp`, commit `d5bffb3dc0`, with an isolated temporary `HOME`, `OPENCLAW_HOME`, and `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Created a temporary Codex-compatible bundle containing `.codex-plugin/plugin.json`, `.mcp.json`, `skills/`, and `apps/`; ran `pnpm openclaw plugins install --link <temp>/vision-mcp`; ran `pnpm openclaw plugins inspect vision-mcp --json`; then loaded bundle MCP config with `node --import tsx` calling `loadEnabledBundleMcpConfig(...)` against the isolated home/config.

Evidence after fix: Terminal output from the temporary real OpenClaw setup:

```text
Proof root: /var/folders/.../openclaw-bundle-proof.gE7Fjt
$ node scripts/run-node.mjs plugins install --link /var/folders/.../openclaw-bundle-proof.gE7Fjt/vision-mcp
Linked plugin path: /var/folders/.../openclaw-bundle-proof.gE7Fjt/vision-mcp
Restart the gateway to load plugins.
$ node scripts/run-node.mjs plugins inspect vision-mcp --json
{
  "diagnostics": [],
  "command": "npx",
  "args": [
    "-y",
    "@vision-mcp/cli@latest",
    "serve",
    "--apps-root",
    "/private/var/folders/.../openclaw-bundle-proof.gE7Fjt/vision-mcp/apps"
  ],
  "cwdEndsWith": true,
  "toolFilter": {
    "include": [
      "capsule.*",
      "vision_map.snapshot",
      "vision_map.list_apps",
      "vision_map.list_workflows",
      "vision_map.describe_workflow",
      "vision_map.describe",
      "vision_map.detect_state",
      "vision_map.list_actions",
      "vision_map.describe_action",
      "vision_map.perform_action",
      "vision_map.run_workflow",
      "vision_map.verify",
      "vision_map.repair_minimal",
      "vision_map.export_trace"
    ],
    "exclude": [
      "vision_map.click_at",
      "vision_map.type_text",
      "vision_map.press_key",
      "vision_map.scroll",
      "vision_map.init",
      "vision_map.apply_patch",
      "vision_map.add_control",
      "vision_map.commit_*",
      "vision_map.harvest_session"
    ]
  }
}
```

Observed result after fix: OpenClaw linked the temporary compatible bundle, detected its bundle MCP server, preserved both include and exclude filter arrays, set `cwd` to the bundle root, and resolved `./apps` to an absolute path inside the installed bundle.

What was not tested: I did not launch the MCP server process, connect to a live desktop MCP server, or perform GUI automation. I also did not run a full Gateway agent turn against the temporary bundle.

Proof limitations or environment constraints: The proof uses a temporary local compatible bundle and the real OpenClaw plugin installer/loader, but the MCP server command itself is not started because this PR changes documentation and loader regression coverage, not MCP runtime execution behavior.

## Tests and validation

Commands run:

```text
node scripts/test-projects.mjs -- src/plugins/bundle-mcp.test.ts --reporter=verbose
pnpm format:check src/plugins/bundle-mcp.test.ts
git diff --check
pnpm check:docs
codex review --base origin/main
```

Regression coverage added: `src/plugins/bundle-mcp.test.ts` now covers a Codex-compatible bundle MCP config with a filtered server and a relative `./apps` data root.

Validation results:

```text
src/plugins/bundle-mcp.test.ts: 1 file passed, 8 tests passed
pnpm format:check src/plugins/bundle-mcp.test.ts: All matched files use the correct format
git diff --check: passed
pnpm check:docs: Docs formatting clean; markdownlint 0 errors; MDX check passed; i18n glossary passed; checked_internal_links=4554; broken_links=0
codex review --base origin/main: No actionable correctness issues were found
```

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`): No runtime behavior change; docs now describe existing bundle MCP filter behavior.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`): No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`): No runtime change. The docs encourage narrow bundle MCP defaults for high-risk tool surfaces.

What is the highest-risk area? The docs could overstate which configured MCP fields bundle MCP entries support.

How is that risk mitigated? Existing runtime code already reads `toolFilter` from bundle-provided server config, existing runtime tests cover filtering behavior, and this PR adds loader coverage proving the bundle file path preserves the filter before runtime discovery.

## Current review state

AI-assisted: yes.

Next action: maintainer review.

target.title: fix: recover terminal session status on visible inbound turns
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #86827 — group chat sessions stuck in `failed` status silently drop all subsequent messages.

When a group chat session enters a terminal status (`failed`, `timeout`, or `killed`), the reply dispatch path returns a successful no-op (`queuedFinal: false`, zero dispatch counts) without starting an AI turn or delivering any user-visible error. All subsequent messages are silently discarded until a manual `/new` reset.

## Root Cause

Three independent mechanisms conspire to create the silent drop:

1. **Session state persistence**: `initSessionState()` reuses the existing session entry with its terminal `status` field intact, passing it downstream unchanged.
2. **Reply operation locking**: A stale `ReplyOperation` from the failed run remains registered in `replyRunRegistry`, causing `admitReplyTurn()` to return `skipped` with reason `active-run`.
3. **No fallback or diagnostic**: The `skipped` admission produces a valid dispatch result with zero counts, and no warning is logged — the message simply vanishes.

## Changes

### 1. Auto-recover terminal sessions (`session.ts`)

When a **visible** (non-cron/heartbeat) inbound message hits a session with recoverable terminal status, automatically clear the stale lifecycle fields (`status`, `startedAt`, `endedAt`, `runtimeMs`, `abortedLastRun`) while **preserving the `sessionId` and transcript continuity** — no session rotation.

### 2. Clear stale reply operations with race guard (`dispatch-from-config.ts`)

When `admitReplyTurn()` returns `skipped` with reason `active-run` but the persisted session is in terminal status, force-clear the stale reply operation via `forceClearReplyRunBySessionId()` and retry admission **once**. This breaks the deadlock without risking infinite retry loops.

**Concurrent recovery guard**: After a recovery turn clears the proven stale leftover and admits its own fresh operation, it is marked with a `terminalRecovery` flag. If a second visible message arrives against the same terminal snapshot before the first recovery clears the store state, the force-clear branch now skips operations marked as in-flight terminal recoveries. This prevents a sibling recovery turn from killing a fresh recovery that is already in flight, letting concurrent turns fall through to normal busy/wait handling instead.

### 3. Agent API path (`agent.ts`)

Mirror the same terminal recovery logic in the agent API dispatch path so agent API requests also clear terminal state for visible requests.

### 4. Diagnostics (`kernel.ts`, `types.ts`)

Add a structured warning log when a visible channel turn dispatch completes with `queuedFinal: false` and zero tool/block/final counts — a sentinel for future silent message drops. Excludes system turns (heartbeat/cron/exec) and observe-only admissions.

## Real behavior proof

Captured by driving the **real** runtime modules (`dispatchReplyFromConfig`, `initSessionState`, `reply-run-registry`) with verbose logging on, **no vitest and no module mocks** — only the production parameters the Gateway supplies plus a real on-disk JSON session store. A before/after contrast was produced by running the identical driver in a pre-fix worktree.

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: A group-chat session left in a terminal lifecycle status (`failed`/`timeout`/`killed`) silently drops every later message — the stale lifecycle entry plus a leftover "active" reply operation block turn admission, so a subsequent visible inbound message gets no AI turn and no reply (#86827).
- **Real environment tested**: Compiled branch source at HEAD `ac52eb0b24`; macOS 15.3.1 (Darwin 24.3.0, arm64), Node `v24.10.0`. Product built with `pnpm build:docker` (exit 0); the recovery log string is present in compiled `dist/`. Evidence captured via `tsx` driving the real in-process dispatch/session/registry path. Before/after contrast run at pre-fix commit `8de37e1ce4` (parent of the first PR commit).
- **Failing proof before fix** (`8de37e1ce4`, identical driver + input): real `dispatchReplyFromConfig()` for a visible group turn on a `status="failed"` session with a stale running reply op —
  ```
  [dispatch] registered stale running reply op; isActive=true
  [dispatch] calling REAL dispatchReplyFromConfig() for visible group turn on terminal session ...
  [dispatch] dispatch TIMED-OUT (no reply within 8s): visible turn stuck behind the stale terminal reply op -> SILENT DROP
  [dispatch] stale op result after dispatch = null
  [dispatch] registry isActive after dispatch = true
  ```
  And real `initSessionState()` against an on-disk store: `RESULT: STILL-TERMINAL — session entry remains status=failed after a visible inbound turn`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  ```
  NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=8192 pnpm build:docker
  export OPENCLAW_HOME=$(mktemp -d) OPENCLAW_DISABLE_BUNDLED_PLUGINS=1 \
         OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1 OPENCLAW_SKIP_PROVIDERS=1 OPENCLAW_SKIP_CRON=1
  node_modules/.bin/tsx ./_dispatch_driver.mts   # drives real dispatchReplyFromConfig()
  node_modules/.bin/tsx ./_proof_driver.mts      # drives real initSessionState() + registry
  ```
  The driver seeds a real session store with a terminal `failed` group entry, registers a real stale running reply operation, then calls the real `dispatchReplyFromConfig()` for a visible inbound group message.
- **Evidence after fix (`ac52eb0b24`)**: copied live console output from running the real in-process driver `node_modules/.bin/tsx ./_dispatch_driver.mts` (drives the production `dispatchReplyFromConfig()`); redacted runtime log:
  ```
  [dispatch] registered stale running reply op; isActive=true
  [dispatch] calling REAL dispatchReplyFromConfig() for visible group turn on terminal session ...
  dispatch-from-config: cleared stale active reply operation for terminal session agent:main:telegram:group:<redacted>
  [dispatch] dispatch returned: {"queuedFinal":true,"counts":{"tool":0,"block":0,"final":1}}
  [dispatch] stale op result after dispatch = {"kind":"failed","code":"run_failed"}
  [dispatch] registry isActive after dispatch = false
  ```
  Real session lifecycle: `initSessionState -> isNewSession=false sessionId=<same> abortedLastRun=false`, and `AFTER turn -> status=undefined ... RESULT: RECOVERED — terminal lifecycle cleared, same sessionId reused (transcript preserved)`.
- **Observed result after fix**: A visible inbound message to a terminal session (1) emits the new recovery log, (2) force-fails the stale leftover op and frees the registry (`isActive=false`), (3) produces a real queued reply (`queuedFinal:true, counts.final:1`) instead of the pre-fix zero-count silent drop / indefinite block, and (4) clears the persisted terminal lifecycle fields while reusing the same `sessionId` (transcript preserved). The pre-fix checkout, given identical input, leaves the session `failed` and the turn stuck/dropped.
- **What was not tested**: The full Docker image build failed at the `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile` layer due to transient npm-registry CDN errors fetching optional native packages (network, not OOM and not code), so no end-to-end live channel socket round-trip was performed; the proof drives the real in-process dispatch/session/registry runtime that a channel turn funnels into. The `agent.ts` Gateway RPC path and the `kernel.ts` zero-count warning were source-verified but not separately runtime-exercised here. Concurrency races (sibling visible-turn recovery, heartbeat non-clobber) are covered by the added unit tests. Architecture: arm64 (macOS) only; CI truth is Linux x64 Node 24.

## Tests

- **session.test.ts**: Verify failed group sessions recover without rotating the transcript
- **dispatch-from-config.test.ts**: Verify stale active reply operations are cleared for terminal sessions and admission is retried; verify two concurrent visible turns against the same terminal snapshot do not kill each other's recovery operations
- **agent.test.ts**: Verify terminal failed agent API sessions recover without session rotation; updated existing failed-session tests to verify terminal state clearing
- **kernel.test.ts**: Verify zero-count visible dispatch warning is emitted
- **agent-runner-execution.test.ts / agent-runner-memory.test.ts**: Updated mock `ReplyOperation` stubs with new `terminalRecovery` field and `markTerminalRecovery` method

All focused tests pass across 2 Vitest shards (512 cases).
