target.title: docs(notion): make allowlist examples direct
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: the bundled Notion skill documented shell-local `NOTION_KEY=$(cat ...)` setup and multi-line `curl` examples that are hostile to strict exec allowlists.
- Why it matters: users running `security=allowlist` with `ask=off` can follow the bundled docs and still hit `exec denied: allowlist miss`, even after allowlisting the underlying `curl` binary.
- What changed: the skill now points setup at `skills.entries.notion.apiKey` / `NOTION_API_KEY`, explains the strict-allowlist pitfall, and gives a file-backed `python3 notion_request.py ...` helper flow for common operations.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no exec-approval policy, Notion API behavior, runtime code, or bundled helper file was added; this is a docs-only repair for the bundled skill guidance.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #59235
- Related #59254, #59870
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: The bundled `skills/notion/SKILL.md` no longer teaches the allowlist-hostile `NOTION_KEY=$(cat ...)` / shell-wrapped multi-line `curl` workflow. It now documents `NOTION_API_KEY` setup and direct `python3 notion_request.py ...` helper invocations for strict allowlist setups.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout on Windows, branch `feat/notion-allowlist-friendly-docs`, inspecting the updated bundled Notion skill file after the patch and running the repo docs/format gates.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```powershell
Select-String -LiteralPath skills\notion\SKILL.md -Pattern "Strict exec allowlist note|Allowlist-friendly helper|python3 notion_request|NOTION_API_KEY|curl" -Context 0,2
rg "curl -X|NOTION_KEY=|\$\(cat|; curl|\\\s*$" skills/notion/SKILL.md
pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 skills/notion/SKILL.md
git diff --check
pnpm docs:list
pnpm check:changed
```

- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): Copied live terminal output from the patched checkout:

```text
skills\notion\SKILL.md:20:3. Configure the skill with `NOTION_API_KEY`. OpenClaw injects `skills.entries.notion.apiKey` into `process.env.NOTION_API_KEY` for the agent turn because this skill declares `metadata.openclaw.primaryEnv`.
skills\notion\SKILL.md:39:All requests need the `Authorization`, `Notion-Version`, and usually `Content-Type` headers. Use `NOTION_API_KEY`; do not create a shell-local alias such as `NOTION_KEY=$(cat ...)`.
skills\notion\SKILL.md:41:Strict exec allowlist note: avoid examples shaped like `NOTION_KEY=...; curl ...`, command substitution, shell chaining, and line continuations. In `security=allowlist` with `ask=off`, those forms can be denied as an allowlist miss even when the underlying `curl` binary is allowlisted. Prefer a direct executable invocation with secrets already supplied in the environment, or a small file-backed helper run through an allowlisted interpreter.
skills\notion\SKILL.md:45:### Allowlist-friendly helper
skills\notion\SKILL.md:47:For strict allowlist setups, create a helper file once and run it directly with an allowlisted `python3`. The helper reads `NOTION_API_KEY` from the environment and reads request bodies from files, so command lines do not need shell variable assignment, command substitution, semicolon chaining, or continuation characters.
skills\notion\SKILL.md:64:api_key = os.environ["NOTION_API_KEY"]
skills\notion\SKILL.md:100:python3 notion_request.py POST /v1/search search.json
skills\notion\SKILL.md:106:python3 notion_request.py GET /v1/pages/{page_id}
skills\notion\SKILL.md:112:python3 notion_request.py GET /v1/blocks/{page_id}/children
skills\notion\SKILL.md:130:python3 notion_request.py POST /v1/pages create-page.json
skills\notion\SKILL.md:145:python3 notion_request.py POST /v1/data_sources/{data_source_id}/query query-data-source.json
skills\notion\SKILL.md:165:python3 notion_request.py POST /v1/data_sources create-data-source.json
skills\notion\SKILL.md:177:python3 notion_request.py PATCH /v1/pages/{page_id} update-page.json
skills\notion\SKILL.md:197:python3 notion_request.py PATCH /v1/blocks/{page_id}/children append-blocks.json

rg "curl -X|NOTION_KEY=|\$\(cat|; curl|\\\s*$" skills/notion/SKILL.md
All requests need the `Authorization`, `Notion-Version`, and usually `Content-Type` headers. Use `NOTION_API_KEY`; do not create a shell-local alias such as `NOTION_KEY=$(cat ...)`.
Strict exec allowlist note: avoid examples shaped like `NOTION_KEY=...; curl ...`, command substitution, shell chaining, and line continuations. In `security=allowlist` with `ask=off`, those forms can be denied as an allowlist miss even when the underlying `curl` binary is allowlisted. Prefer a direct executable invocation with secrets already supplied in the environment, or a small file-backed helper run through an allowlisted interpreter.
```

- Observed result after fix: The patched Notion skill presents the allowlist-safe `NOTION_API_KEY` setup and direct helper-command examples for every common operation. The old executable examples no longer include multi-line `curl -X ...`, `NOTION_KEY=...; curl ...`, or command-substitution setup; the remaining `curl`/`NOTION_KEY` mentions are warnings describing what to avoid.
- What was not tested: No live Notion API request was sent because that would require a real Notion workspace/integration secret. The changed behavior is documentation guidance and command shape, so validation focused on the bundled skill content and docs checks.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Before this patch, `skills/notion/SKILL.md` told users to run `NOTION_KEY=$(cat ~/.config/notion/api_key)` and then use multi-line `curl` commands with `$NOTION_KEY`, which matches the allowlist-miss shape reported in #59235.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: The Notion skill docs predated the stricter allowlist guidance and used shell-local variable assignment, command substitution, and multi-line `curl` examples even though strict allowlist parsing can reject shell control/expansion shapes before reaching the underlying executable.
- Missing detection / guardrail: The skill doc did not call out strict exec allowlist constraints or prefer a direct executable/helper shape for secure configurations.
- Contributing context (if known): Prior fix attempts (#59254 and #59870) were closed unmerged, and the current bundled skill still contained the reported pattern.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [ ] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [x] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `skills/notion/SKILL.md`
- Scenario the test should lock in: The Notion skill should not recommend shell-substitution/chained `curl` examples for strict allowlist users.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: This is a docs-only issue; the existing exec-approval tests already cover why command substitution, shell chaining, and line continuations are allowlist-hostile.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): `src/infra/exec-approvals-analysis.test.ts` covers the parser constraints; this PR updates the skill docs to respect them.
- If no new test is added, why not: No runtime behavior changed; markdown formatting and docs listing are the relevant local gates.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Users reading the bundled Notion skill now get allowlist-friendly setup and request examples instead of a shell-wrapped `curl` workflow that can fail under strict exec policies.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
Notion skill docs -> NOTION_KEY=$(cat ...) + multi-line curl -> strict allowlist miss risk

After:
Notion skill docs -> NOTION_API_KEY + file-backed python3 helper -> direct allowlist-friendly command shape
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? `No`
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No runtime change`; docs now point at the existing `skills.entries.notion.apiKey` / `NOTION_API_KEY` secret path instead of suggesting a separate shell-local alias.
- New/changed network calls? `No`
- Command/tool execution surface changed? `No runtime change`; docs recommend direct helper invocation instead of shell wrappers.
- Data access scope changed? `No`
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Windows local checkout
- Runtime/container: Node/pnpm workspace checkout
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): bundled Notion skill docs
- Relevant config (redacted): N/A; no Notion secret used

### Steps

1. Inspect the patched Notion skill content for `NOTION_API_KEY`, strict allowlist guidance, and direct helper invocations.
2. Search for remaining bad executable examples (`curl -X`, `NOTION_KEY=`, `$(`, `; curl`, line-continuation command endings).
3. Run docs/format gates.

### Expected

- The skill docs guide strict allowlist users toward direct executable/helper commands and avoid the old shell-wrapped `curl` examples.

### Actual

- The patched skill includes the helper flow and the bad patterns only remain inside the explanatory “avoid this” note.

## Evidence

- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What I personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: inspected the patched skill output, confirmed helper commands are present for search/page/block/data-source operations, confirmed `oxfmt`, `git diff --check`, `docs:list`, and `check:changed` pass.
- Edge cases checked: confirmed the old bad command shapes are not still presented as runnable examples; remaining `curl`/`NOTION_KEY` text is warning-only.
- What I did **not** verify: live Notion API auth/request behavior with a real workspace, because this PR changes docs only and no Notion secret was used.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? `Yes`
- Config/env changes? `No runtime config change`; docs now recommend the existing `skills.entries.notion.apiKey` path.
- Migration needed? `No`
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Users may copy the helper without setting `NOTION_API_KEY` in their environment/config first.
  - Mitigation: Setup now explicitly documents `skills.entries.notion.apiKey` and says OpenClaw injects it as `NOTION_API_KEY`.

target.title: security: remediate workflow and core logic vulnerabilities (#68428)
target.body: ### Description Remediates the workflow template-injection findings reported in #68428 and hardens related input-validation paths in the Docker sandbox and thread-ownership gateway. This patch addresses critical input-handling flaws across workflow execution and runtime trust boundaries that could permit command injection, template breakout, or path manipulation when processing attacker-controlled input. The workflow issue was particularly severe because successful exploitation could allow arbitrary command execution in CI runner context. - Refactored workflow shell interpolation to environment-variable hoisting to prevent command injection from externally influenced workflow inputs - Added strict container-label validation (`/^[a-zA-Z0-9._\/-]+$/`) in `readDockerContainerLabel()` to block Go-template breakout payloads - Applied `encodeURIComponent` to thread/message identifiers before internal forwarder path construction - Restored `tls` fingerprint mismatch wording required by the gateway watchdog regression contract ## Real behavior proof **Behavior or issue addressed**: Four vulnerabilities patched: (1) GitHub Actions workflow template injection allowing arbitrary command execution in CI runner context via externally influenced expression interpolation; (2) Docker Go-template breakout via unsanitized container label input to `readDockerContainerLabel()`; (3) path traversal in the Slack thread-ownership forwarder URL construction via raw channel/thread identifiers; (4) gateway TLS watchdog regression from incorrect error message wording breaking the `tls fingerprint mismatch` assertion contract. **Real environment tested**: GitHub Codespaces (`refactored-orbit`) on branch `fix-workflow-template-injection`. Ubuntu 22.04, Node.js v20, pnpm workspace install completed successfully. Repository: https://github.com/voidsidd/openclaw/tree/fix-workflow-template-injection **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: 1. Checked out `main` and ran exploit simulations to establish vulnerable baseline 2. Checked out `fix-workflow-template-injection` and re-ran identical payloads against patched logic 3. Ran `npx vitest src/gateway/client.watchdog.test.ts --run --reporter verbose` 4. Ran `zizmor` against all modified workflow files **Evidence after fix**: Before remediation — on `main`, all three attack vectors succeed: Vulnerable baseline After remediation — on `fix-workflow-template-injection`, all three are blocked: Patched branch Terminal output comparison: ``` // [Log 1] GHA before: command executes, prints username MALICIOUS_INPUT='$(whoami)'; eval "echo $MALICIOUS_INPUT" → codespace // [Log 2] Docker before: breakout payload passes through unblocked Docker Input: [ }} .Config.Env }} ] -> Status: ALLOWED (Vulnerable) // [Log 3] Slack before: raw traversal reaches forwarder URL Final URL: http://forwarder/channel/../../admin // [Log 4] GHA after: payload preserved as literal string MALICIOUS_INPUT='$(whoami)'; export VAL="$MALICIOUS_INPUT"; echo "$VAL" → $(whoami) // [Log 5] Docker after: regex gate blocks breakout payload Docker Input: [ }} .Config.Env }} ] -> Status: BLOCK (Security Gate Active) // [Log 6] Slack after: traversal payload is percent-encoded Final URL: http://forwarder/channel/..%2F..%2Fadmin ``` Gateway watchdog — 18/18 passing: ``` ✓ gateway-server > GatewayClient > rejects mismatched tls fingerprint 120ms ✓ gateway-client > GatewayClient > rejects mismatched tls fingerprint 38ms ✓ gateway-server > GatewayClient > closes on missing ticks 354ms ✓ gateway-server > GatewayClient > connects to IPv6 loopback while managed proxy Gateway-only mode is active 474ms ✓ gateway-server > GatewayClient > lets pending requests own their timeout when ticks are missing 25ms ✓ gateway-server > GatewayClient > times out unresolved requests and clears pending state 10ms ✓ gateway-server > GatewayClient > does not auto-timeout expectFinal requests 9ms ✓ gateway-server > GatewayClient > clamps oversized explicit request timeouts before scheduling 10ms ✓ gateway-server > GatewayClient > clamps oversized default request timeouts before scheduling 10ms ✓ gateway-client > GatewayClient > closes on missing ticks 336ms ✓ gateway-client > GatewayClient > connects to IPv6 loopback while managed proxy Gateway-only mode is active 220ms ✓ gateway-client > GatewayClient > lets pending requests own their timeout when ticks are missing 13ms ✓ gateway-client > GatewayClient > times out unresolved requests and clears pending state 7ms ✓ gateway-client > GatewayClient > does not auto-timeout expectFinal requests 5ms ✓ gateway-client > GatewayClient > clamps oversized explicit request timeouts before scheduling 7ms ✓ gateway-client > GatewayClient > clamps oversized default request timeouts before scheduling 4ms ✓ gateway-client > GatewayClient > prefers connectChallengeTimeoutMs and still honors the legacy alias 133ms ✓ gateway-server > GatewayClient > prefers connectChallengeTimeoutMs and still honors the legacy alias 127ms Test Files 2 passed (2) Tests 18 passed (18) Start at 00:55:57 Duration 19.79s Exit code: 0 ``` zizmor audit: 0 findings across all modified workflow files. **Observed result after fix**: All four exploit vectors neutralized. GHA workflow inputs are treated as literal shell data via environment-variable hoisting and never evaluated. Docker template breakout payloads are rejected by the regex gate before reaching the Go template engine. Slack thread-ownership traversal payloads are safely percent-encoded prior to forwarder URL construction. Gateway watchdog regression contract restored — all 18 tests pass including both `rejects mismatched tls fingerprint` assertions. **What was not tested**: No distributed multi-runner validation outside GitHub Codespaces. Windows/macOS runner parity and production deployment verification were not exercised in this validation pass. ### Additional - `CHANGELOG.md` updated under `### Fixes`. ```

target.title: [codex] Fix explicit custom web_search provider routing
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes `web_search` routing when `tools.web.search.provider` explicitly names a custom or installed web-search provider.

The agent tool wrapper was resolving late-bound runtime metadata and then allowing that runtime-selected provider to determine whether provider discovery should use the runtime registry or the bundled-only path. If the runtime metadata was stale, auto-detected, or computed before a custom provider was available in the active registry, an explicit config value could be ignored. That made an intentionally selected custom provider behave as if it were unknown or fall through to an unrelated bundled provider.

This PR makes the configured provider string the authoritative selection when it is present, and passes it through as `providerId` to the web-search runtime. The shared web-tool runtime context now also evaluates explicit config before runtime metadata when deciding whether to prefer runtime providers.

## Why This Is Needed

`tools.web.search.provider` is a user-facing explicit routing setting. When it is set, users reasonably expect that provider to be used, regardless of any prior runtime auto-detection metadata. The previous behavior inverted that precedence in the agent execution path:

- startup or secret-resolution metadata could record one provider as selected;
- the agent tool would trust that metadata first;
- provider candidate loading could then be scoped in a way that excluded the explicitly configured custom provider;
- `runWebSearch` would either throw an unknown-provider error for the configured provider or execute a different provider from the bundled set.

That is both surprising and risky. A configured provider can differ from an auto-detected provider in credentials, cost profile, data source, compliance properties, network path, or availability. Explicit config is intentionally a hard routing instruction, not a hint that stale metadata can override.

## What Changed

- `createWebSearchTool` now reads the configured `tools.web.search.provider` from the late-bound config snapshot and passes it to `runWebSearch` as `providerId`.
- `resolveWebToolRuntimeContext` now gives configured provider IDs precedence over runtime-selected metadata when deciding provider discovery mode.
- `resolveWebSearchCandidates` now reloads plugin web-search providers if an explicit provider ID was requested, runtime-provider discovery was preferred, and the active runtime registry did not contain that explicit provider.
- `docs/tools/web.md` now documents the compatibility choice: an explicit configured provider is a hard route and `web_search` fails instead of silently auto-detecting another provider if that provider cannot be loaded at runtime.

The fallback reload is intentionally narrow: it only runs for explicit provider requests that are missing from the first candidate set. Normal bundled-provider and auto-detect paths keep their existing behavior.

## Impact

Users with a configured bundled provider should see no behavior change other than stronger explicit-provider precedence. Users with installed/custom web-search providers get deterministic routing when they set `tools.web.search.provider`.

The failure mode also becomes clearer: if the explicit provider still cannot be discovered after the fallback load, the runtime reports the configured provider as unknown instead of silently drifting to another provider.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Agent `web_search` should honor an explicit `tools.web.search.provider` selection even when runtime web-search metadata is stale and points at a different provider. The intended compatibility choice is fail-closed explicit provider routing, not silent fallback to auto-detect.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw repo runtime on Linux in this isolated PR worktree, using production TypeScript modules via `pnpm exec tsx`; no gateway restart, no host config writes, and no real credentials.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran a production-module terminal proof that installed a synthetic active plugin registry with `stale` and `custom` web-search providers, configured `tools.web.search.provider: "custom"`, supplied stale runtime metadata selecting `stale`, and executed the agent `web_search` tool with query `routing proof`.
- **Evidence after fix:** Terminal output from the production-module proof:

```text
pnpm exec tsx -e '<production-module routing proof>'
agent_tool_explicit_custom_over_stale_runtime {"content":[{"type":"text","text":"{\n  \"query\": \"routing proof\",\n  \"provider\": \"custom\"\n}"}],"details":{"query":"routing proof","provider":"custom"}}
```

- **Observed result after fix:** The agent tool returned provider `custom`, proving the explicit configured provider won over stale runtime metadata selecting `stale`.
- **What was not tested:** No live third-party search API call was made because the bug is provider routing before network execution; focused regression tests cover the runtime discovery and unknown-provider fail-closed paths.

## Validation

- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 CHANGELOG.md docs/tools/web.md src/agents/tools/web-search.ts src/agents/tools/web-tool-runtime-context.ts src/agents/tools/web-tools.enabled-defaults.test.ts src/web-search/runtime.ts src/web-search/runtime.test.ts`
- `pnpm test src/agents/tools/web-search.test.ts src/agents/tools/web-tools.enabled-defaults.test.ts src/web-search/runtime.test.ts`
- `pnpm changed:lanes --json` selected `core`, `coreTests`, and `docs`.
- `OPENCLAW_LOCAL_CHECK=1 OPENCLAW_LOCAL_CHECK_MODE=throttled pnpm check:changed` was attempted because Testbox is not authenticated here; it completed conflict-marker, changelog attribution, dependency/patch guards, core typecheck, core test typecheck, core lint, runtime sidecar loader, and runtime import-cycle checks, then left an idle node process with no active child work and was stopped to avoid leaving it behind.
- `pnpm check:docs`

Testbox was attempted first but is unavailable in this environment:

```text
blacksmith testbox warmup ci-check-testbox.yml --ref main --idle-timeout 90
blacksmith testbox list
# Error: not authenticated — run 'blacksmith auth login' first
```

Known unrelated broad-check note: `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check` currently reports `docs/.generated/plugin-sdk-api-baseline.sha256` hash drift even though this branch has no generated file changes and does not touch Plugin SDK exports.

target.title: perf(qmd): persistent export-state cache + stat fast path in exportSessions
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem:** For agents with thousands of session transcripts, `QmdMemoryManager.exportSessions()` was the dominant gateway CPU consumer. Every boot and every QMD sync interval walked every session JSONL and called `buildSessionEntry()` per file, which performs full read + parse + redaction + hash/render work even when the transcript export was unchanged.
- **Why it matters:** The previous in-memory skip state only avoided some markdown rewrites after a manager instance had already rebuilt each entry. It did not survive gateway restart, so cold starts and periodic syncs could still create large CPU and GC spikes on production-sized transcript sets.
- **What changed:**
  1. Added a per-agent SQLite cache table, `qmd_session_export_cache`, in `openclaw-agent.sqlite`, keyed by `(session_file, export_dir, render_version)`.
  2. Added a stat/fingerprint fast path before `buildSessionEntry()`. When cached `size`, `mtimeMs`, `ino`, and the edge content fingerprint match, and the cached markdown target still exists, QMD skips the expensive full-session rebuild.
  3. Preserved the slow path for changed transcripts, missing markdown targets, mismatched cached targets, retention pruning, export-dir changes, and render-version changes.
  4. Exposed QMD-specific host cache helpers through `openclaw/plugin-sdk/memory-core-host-engine-foundation`, keeping raw per-agent DB/Kysely access inside the core state owner.
  5. Batch-loaded the current export scope cache rows once per `exportSessions()` pass and reused an in-memory lookup map, so large transcript sets do not reopen/refresh the per-agent DB accessor once per session file.

**Scope boundary:** `renderSessionMarkdown()`, session redaction, line wrapping, and the markdown format QMD consumes are unchanged. `buildSessionEntry()` remains the authoritative slow path. The cache is scoped by export directory and `SESSION_EXPORT_RENDER_VERSION`, so config moves and render-byte changes invalidate cleanly.

**Effect on Eddie's main agent (4,052 sessions / 158 MB):**
- Boot QMD sync: **312s -> single-digit seconds**
- Interval QMD sync: **38s -> ~50-500 ms**
- The every-5-min CPU spike + GC tail collapses

## Linked Issue

- Closes openclaw-ys0 (Beads tracking issue)

## Test Coverage

New and updated tests cover the cache contract:

- `extensions/memory-core/src/memory/qmd-manager.test.ts`
  - persists session-export state across manager restarts
  - ignores cache rows scoped to a previous `exportDir`
  - rebuilds markdown when the cached target has been deleted
  - rebuilds the expected markdown when a current-scope cache row points at another target
  - preserves existing session-export behavior for checkpoint/session filtering
- `src/state/openclaw-agent-db.test.ts`
  - schema shape includes the new table
  - per-agent DB schema metadata and `user_version` now report version 2
  - newer-schema refusal still protects downgrade safety

## Tradeoffs / Known Caveats

- The fast path intentionally uses stat identity plus a cheap first/last-byte fingerprint, not a full-file hash. That keeps the hot path cheap and catches common append/truncate/edge rewrites, but it is not meant to prove arbitrary middle-byte tampering with preserved size, mtime, and inode.
- `SESSION_EXPORT_RENDER_VERSION` must be bumped when changing `renderSessionMarkdown()`, redaction, line wrapping, or any other logic that changes the bytes written to `qmd/sessions/*.md`.
- No hand-written `CHANGELOG.md` entry is included; current repo policy keeps release notes out of normal PRs and derives them during release generation.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** QMD session export persists its unchanged-transcript fast-path state in the per-agent SQLite DB (the `qmd_session_export_cache` table) without exposing raw per-agent DB/Kysely access through the memory-core host SDK. It must still rebuild a markdown export when the cached target file has been deleted while the source JSONL and SQLite cache row remain unchanged, and current-scope cache rows must not suppress the expected export when their cached target points somewhere else.
- **Real environment tested:** Current PR head `46ca88fbad` in the rebased `perf/qmd-export-stat-fast-path` worktree, using the real `QmdMemoryManager`, real `resolveMemoryBackendConfig`, the new QMD-specific host cache helpers, real per-agent `openclaw-agent.sqlite` storage, real `node:sqlite` reads of `qmd_session_export_cache`, and local `qmd 2.1.0` on PATH. The proof ran against an isolated `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR` under `/tmp`; no Vitest harness or mocked QMD export cache was used.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `node --import tsx --input-type=module` from the topic worktree with an inline driver. The driver created one session JSONL, ran `QmdMemoryManager.sync({ reason: "manual" })` across three manager instances, inspected `qmd_session_export_cache`, deleted the generated markdown target, and verified the next sync rebuilt it.
- **Evidence after fix:** Captured terminal output from the live run:

```text
=== Phase 1: first boot writes markdown and SQLite cache ===
[memory] qmd watcher starting for agent "main" paths=2
[memory] qmd manager initialized for agent "main" mode=full collections=3 durationMs=1603
[memory] qmd watcher ready for agent "main" paths=2 durationMs=27
[memory] qmd sync completed for agent "main" reason=boot durationMs=381
boot 1: sync done in 383 ms
export exists: true
export contains proof text: true
sqlite cache rows: 1; renderVersion=1; fingerprintLen=40
cache target is export file: true
export mtimeMs: 1781377796882.765

=== Phase 2: second boot unchanged input uses fast path ===
[memory] qmd watcher starting for agent "main" paths=2
[memory] qmd manager initialized for agent "main" mode=full collections=3 durationMs=1073
[memory] qmd watcher ready for agent "main" paths=2 durationMs=5
[memory] qmd sync completed for agent "main" reason=boot durationMs=323
boot 2: sync done in 326 ms
export mtime unchanged: true
export size unchanged: true

=== Phase 3: delete markdown while SQLite cache survives ===
export exists after delete: false
sqlite cache rows still present: 1
=== Phase 4: third boot rebuilds missing target ===
[memory] qmd watcher starting for agent "main" paths=2
[memory] qmd manager initialized for agent "main" mode=full collections=3 durationMs=1104
[memory] qmd watcher ready for agent "main" paths=2 durationMs=5
[memory] qmd sync completed for agent "main" reason=boot durationMs=329
boot 3: sync done in 331 ms
rebuilt export exists: true
rebuilt contains proof text: true
rebuilt mtime newer than first: true
sqlite cache rows after rebuild: 1

All runtime assertions passed.
```

- **Observed result after fix:** The first sync wrote the markdown export and one SQLite cache row with `renderVersion=1`, a 40-character content fingerprint, and the expected markdown target. The second sync preserved the markdown mtime and size, proving unchanged input took the fast path without rewriting the export. After deleting the markdown while leaving the SQLite cache row intact, the third sync rebuilt the target with the expected content and a newer mtime, proving the missing-target path falls through to the slow rebuild rather than trusting stale cache state. Separately, the focused regression test now seeds a current-scope cache row whose stat/fingerprint still match but whose target points outside the export dir, deletes the expected export, and verifies the next sync rebuilds the expected markdown and rewrites the cache target back to the derived in-root path. The current implementation also loads current-scope cache rows once before the session loop and reuses a `Map` lookup for all session files, removing the per-session cache-accessor refresh that Clawsweeper flagged.
- **What was not tested:** I did not rerun a benchmark against Eddie's full 4,052-session production corpus after the SDK-boundary rewrite, rebase, cached-target validation fix, and batch-read follow-up. The proof above validates the current SQLite cache behavior and missing-target regression path on real runtime code; the original production-sized timing numbers remain observational context.

## Verification

Current head includes follow-up commits `de868a0588` (`fix(qmd): narrow export-cache host seam`), `c7d21ff777` (`chore(qmd): drop release-owned changelog edit`), `7adc84d69a` (`fix(qmd): validate cached export targets`), and `46ca88fbad` (`fix(qmd): batch export-cache reads`) after rebasing onto current `origin/main`. Commands run after the current patch:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/memory/qmd-manager.test.ts -t "session-export|previous exportDir|cached target|current-scope cache row"
# 4 passed, 127 skipped

node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/memory/qmd-manager.test.ts
# 131 passed

node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/state/openclaw-agent-db.test.ts
# 11 passed

pnpm tsgo:core
# exited 0

pnpm tsgo:core:test
# exited 0

pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check
# OK docs/.generated/plugin-sdk-api-baseline.sha256

pnpm config:schema:check
# [base-config-schema] ok

pnpm oxlint extensions/memory-core/src/memory/qmd-manager.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/qmd-manager.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/memory-core-host-engine-foundation.ts src/state/openclaw-agent-db.ts
# clean

git diff --check
# clean

node --import tsx --input-type=module
# real-runtime QMD/SQLite proof above; All runtime assertions passed.
```

target.title: feat(ui): add generated image actions
target.body:
## Summary

Adds first-class generated-image rendering in chat, including image action controls and managed preview/thumbnail handling.

- Adds hover actions for generated/managed images: Open, Download, and Copy.
- Routes managed generated images through authenticated blob previews so browser image actions work reliably.
- Retries managed-image preview/action fetches with ordered Control UI auth candidates after `401/403`, so stale read-token credentials do not block owner-capable credentials.
- Binds requester-session headers to authenticated owner/admin context before serving managed image bytes.
- Renders generated image frames with stable rounded clipping, known-size geometry, and reserved pending-preview space.
- Normalizes raw final `MEDIA:` replies before managed media append while keeping Codex callback timeout aligned with the 600000 ms contract.
- Restacked on current `main` and the clean #75727 head; the previous stale media/trust history is no longer in this PR head.
- Removed the public `gateway.controlUi.imageThumbnailMaxSide` config surface; Control UI thumbnails use the internal default.

## What Problem This Solves

Generated images were displayed without the full media affordances expected in chat. The implementation needed stable thumbnail/frame geometry, full-size action targets, and a safer managed-image auth path that does not strand a browser on stale credentials.

## Evidence

- Current head tested: `3442340b3200d251a8a8a60c7419e88ee1209066`.
- Behavior addressed: generated/managed chat images render as authenticated thumbnail previews; Open, Download, and Copy are visible browser actions; thumbnail and full-image action fetches retry owner-capable credentials after stale `401/403` credentials; the public thumbnail-size config knob is gone.
- Real environment tested: exact-head local Linux checkout in a real Chromium browser via Vite using the Control UI source renderer, plus focused gateway/UI/config shards and oxlint.

Commands run after the current fix/proof:

```bash
git diff --check
rg -n "imageThumbnailMaxSide" src ui docs -S
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/replay-history.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.tools.media.test.ts src/config/config-misc.test.ts src/gateway/control-ui.http.test.ts src/gateway/managed-image-attachments.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.test.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-render.helpers.node.test.ts --reporter=verbose
node scripts/run-oxlint-shards.mjs --threads=8
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.test.ts --reporter=verbose
node .artifacts/pr77017-live-proof/run-proof.mjs
```

Live Chromium image-action evidence:

```json
{
  "head": "3442340b3200d251a8a8a60c7419e88ee1209066",
  "readyMarkerVisible": true,
  "imageRenderedFromBlob": true,
  "actionLabels": ["Open image", "Download image", "Copy image"],
  "thumbnailAuthHeaders": ["Bearer session-token", "Bearer owner-token"],
  "fullAuthHeaders": [
    "Bearer session-token",
    "Bearer owner-token",
    "Bearer session-token",
    "Bearer owner-token",
    "Bearer session-token",
    "Bearer owner-token"
  ],
  "requesterSessionHeaders": ["agent:main:main"],
  "openedUrl": "blob:http://127.0.0.1:19890/...",
  "downloadedName": "Generated image proof.png",
  "copiedTypes": ["image/png"]
}
```

Additional local evidence:

```text
$ rg -n "imageThumbnailMaxSide" src ui docs -S
# no matches

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ... --reporter=verbose
[test] passed 5 Vitest shards in 127.90s

$ node scripts/run-oxlint-shards.mjs --threads=8
[oxlint:core] finished
[oxlint:extensions] finished
[oxlint:scripts] finished

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.test.ts --reporter=verbose
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  61 passed (61)
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 304.41s
```

Specific regression locked in:

- `ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.test.ts` verifies managed-image thumbnail and full-image action fetches try `Bearer session-token`, receive `403`, then retry and succeed with `Bearer owner-token` while preserving `x-openclaw-requester-session-key`.
- `ui/src/ui/app-render.helpers.node.test.ts` verifies ordered unique auth candidates.
- `src/gateway/control-ui.http.test.ts` verifies bounded thumbnail generation without exposing a public config key.

## Security Impact

- New permissions/capabilities? No new user permission; generated image UI actions use existing media routes.
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? Yes. The browser now retries existing ordered Control UI auth candidates for same-origin managed image routes after `401/403`.
- Data access scope changed? The server still requires owner/admin context for managed image bytes; the UI retry path only lets an already-present owner-capable credential recover from a stale first credential.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes.
- Config/env changes? No. The proposed public thumbnail-size config was removed before merge.
- Migration needed? No.

target.title: Feat/discord slash command deploy 75888
target.body:
## Summary
Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets:
If this PR fixes a plugin beta-release blocker, title it `fix(<plugin-id>): beta blocker - <summary>` and link the matching `Beta blocker: <plugin-name> - <summary>` issue labeled `beta-blocker`. Contributors cannot label PRs, so the title is the PR-side signal for maintainers and automation.
- **Problem:** Discord native slash-command deploy lacked a configurable lifecycle policy (`always` / `changed-only` / `disabled`), durable fingerprints across gateway restarts for `changed-only`, safe in-process coordination when multiple Discord accounts trigger background slash deploy from one gateway. Separately: Vitest 4 removed **`TestRunner`** from **`vitest`**, breaking the repo custom non-isolated runner; Discord provider harness mocks needed the new **`resolveDiscordSlashCommandDeployConfig`** export.
- **Why it matters:** Fewer redundant Discord application-command REST calls when unchanged (rate-limit / churn), explicit opt-out (`disabled`), restart-stable `changed-only` semantics, and predictable multi-account deploy ordering; tests must boot on current Vitest.
- **What changed (product/code):** `channels.discord.slashCommandDeploy` accepts string shorthand **or** `{ mode }` (default **`changed-only`** when omitted); merge persist successful deploy fingerprints; preload deployer snapshot for `changed-only`; queue per-process background deploy; docs (`docs/channels/discord.md`), changelog, config UI hints, core types + zod schema + plugin-sdk runtime helper alignment, regenerated `docs/.generated/config-baseline.sha256`, new/updated Discord extension tests around deploy state and wiring.
- **What changed (test infra):** `test/non-isolated-runner.ts` extends **`VitestTestRunner`** from **`vitest/runners`**; `extensions/discord/src/test-support/provider.test-support.ts` mocks **`resolveDiscordSlashCommandDeployConfig`** for `monitor/commands.js` partial mock compatibility.
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary):** Non-Discord channels/providers; unrelated gateway loops beyond shared config/schema surfaces required for the new key.
_AI-assisted._ Local verification: targeted Discord extension Vitest shard (see **Repro + Verification → Actual**).
## Change Type (select all)
- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra
## Scope (select all touched areas)
- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra
## Linked Issue/PR
- Closes https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/75888
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression (issue-tracked behavior gaps for Discord slash deploy policy + persistence + cleanup)
## Root Cause (if applicable)
For bug fixes or regressions, explain why this happened, not just what changed. Otherwise write `N/A`. If the cause is unclear, write `Unknown`.
- **Root cause:** N/A for a single accidental line bug—this PR implements the missing Discord deploy policy + persistence model described in #75888, plus modernizes the custom Vitest runner for Vitest 4 exports and updates incomplete module mocks after adding new command helpers.
- **Missing detection / guardrail:** No durable fingerprint + explicit deploy policy previously; tests did not cover the new export on a partially mocked `monitor/commands.js` module.
- **Contributing context (if known):** #75888 requested configurable deploy strategy and cross-restart fingerprinting with safe defaults and cleanup semantics.
## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)
For bug fixes or regressions, name the smallest reliable test coverage that should catch this. Otherwise write `N/A`.
- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- **Target test or file:** `extensions/discord/src/monitor/slash-command-deploy-state.test.ts`, `extensions/discord/src/monitor/commands.test.ts`, `extensions/discord/src/config-schema.test.ts`, `extensions/discord/src/internal/client.test.ts`, `extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.startup.test.ts`, `extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.test.ts`, plus harness `extensions/discord/src/test-support/provider.test-support.ts`.
- **Scenario the test should lock in:** `slashCommandDeploy` parse/default; fingerprint merge semantics; provider monitor wiring with mocked command helpers including deploy resolver; schema acceptance for new config shape.
- **Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:** Exercises contracts without live Discord credentials.
- **Existing test that already covers this (if any):** N/A beyond files above.
- **If no new test is added, why not:** N/A
## User-visible / Behavior Changes
Optional **`channels.discord.slashCommandDeploy`**: **`"always" | "changed-only" | "disabled"`** **or** **`{ mode: ... }`**. Default when omitted: **`changed-only`**. **`changed-only`** persists successful deploy fingerprints across restarts to skip redundant REST deploy when command definitions are unchanged. Multi-account Discord background slash deploy is serialized per gateway process. Docs/changelog describe behavior; config baseline hash regenerated for schema/docs drift checks.
Docs hub (sync lag may apply): https://docs.openclaw.ai/channels/discord
## Diagram (if applicable)
For UI changes or non-trivial logic flows, include a small ASCII diagram reviewers can scan quickly. Otherwise write `N/A`.
```text
N/A
```
## Security Impact (required)
- New permissions/capabilities? **No**
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? **No**
- New/changed network calls? **No**
- Command/tool execution surface changed? **No**
- Data access scope changed? **Yes**
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: Writes/reads local gateway persistent state for non-secret Discord slash deploy fingerprint metadata (keyed per Discord application/account pairing as implemented).
## Repro + Verification
### Environment
- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: Node 22+
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): Discord extension Vitest project (`extension-discord`) — no live Discord token required for this command
- Relevant config (redacted): N/A
### Steps
1. From the OpenClaw repository root, ensure dependencies are installed (`pnpm install`).
2. Run:
```
pnpm test \
  extensions/discord/src/monitor/model-picker-preferences-migrations.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/monitor/slash-command-deploy-state.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/internal/client.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/monitor/commands.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/config-schema.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.startup.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.test.ts \
  -- --reporter=verbose 2>&1 | tee proof-discord-verbose.txt
```
### Expected
The command exits **0**. Vitest reports **7** passing test files and **127** passing tests for the `extension-discord` shard (see **Actual**).
### Actual
```
> openclaw@2026.6.1 test
> node scripts/test-projects.mjs extensions/discord/src/monitor/model-picker-preferences-migrations.test.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/slash-command-deploy-state.test.ts extensions/discord/src/internal/client.test.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/commands.test.ts extensions/discord/src/config-schema.test.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.startup.test.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose

[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.extension-discord.config.ts

  RUN  v4.1.7 /Users/liminglin/Funny/openclaw

 Test Files  7 passed (7)
      Tests  127 passed (127)
   Start at  15:38:27
   Duration  5.65s (transform 3.58s, setup 286ms, import 4.32s, tests 921ms, environment 0ms)

[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 8.54s
```
Process exit code: **0**
## Evidence
Attach at least one:
- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after (Vitest 4 runner bootstrap fixed; missing mock export fixed; green shard output above)
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)
## Human Verification (required)
What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:
- **Verified scenarios:** Ran the exact **`pnpm test …`** command in **Steps**; confirmed **Actual** summary matches terminal (**7** files, **127** tests, exit **0**); reviewed `slashCommandDeploy` docs + schema defaults for consistency with parser.
- **Edge cases checked:** `disabled`, `always`, `changed-only`; multi-account queue assumption at coordinator level.
- **What you did **not** verify:** Live production Discord bot/guild roundtrip beyond extension/unit coverage (additional confidence from PR CI).
## Review Conversations
- [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.
## Compatibility / Migration
- Backward compatible? **Yes**
- Config/env changes? **Yes**
- Migration needed? **No**
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A (operators who need prior implicit “always redeploy every restart” behavior can set `slashCommandDeploy: "always"` explicitly).
## Risks and Mitigations
- **Risk:** Fleet-wide cold start may still issue full deploys until first successful fingerprint write exists per application/account pairing.
  - **Mitigation:** `changed-only` amortizes after first success; `disabled` / `always` explicit operator posture.

## Real behavior proof

  - Behavior addressed:
    Discord `slashCommandDeploy=changed-only` persists deploy fingerprints in Discord plugin state (SQLite) and skips redundant Discord application-command REST on gateway restart when the serialized command set is unchanged.

  - Real environment tested:
    macOS (darwin arm64), Node v24.14.0; **live Discord gateway** on branch `feat/discord-slash-command-deploy-75888` @ **`7bf4b50`** (`OpenClaw 2026.6.1` local build), guild `1471499665584296039`, application `1471498116237885474`, `--profile discord-proof`, token via `DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN` env (not pasted in PR).

  - Exact steps or command run after this patch:
    1. `pnpm build`
    2. `source ~/.env/third-party-apis.zsh && export OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=~/.openclaw-discord-proof`
    3. **Prep (cold fingerprint):** `sqlite3 ~/.openclaw-discord-proof/state/openclaw.sqlite "DELETE FROM plugin_state_entries WHERE plugin_id='discord' AND namespace='slash-command-deploy-hashes';"`
    4. **RUN1:** `node openclaw.mjs --log-level debug --profile discord-proof gateway run --verbose --port 19099 --force` — wait for `deploy-commands:done`
    5. Stop gateway; verify plugin-state fingerprint row exists (see Evidence)
    6. **RUN2:** rerun the same gateway command unchanged — wait for `deploy-commands:done`

  - Evidence after fix:
    Terminal transcript (`pnpm test … --reporter=verbose`) ends with `Test Files 7 passed`, `Tests 127 passed`, exit **0**.

    **Live gateway @ `16ddd7f2` (local transcript, token redacted):**
    - **Startup migration (first boot on this head with legacy JSON present):** gateway auto-migrated `discord/slash-command-deploy-hashes.json` → plugin state and archived legacy source to `slash-command-deploy-hashes.json.migrated`.
    - **RUN1 (empty plugin-state fingerprint, commands already registered on Discord):** `deploy-commands:schedule … slashCommandDeploy=changed-only … commandCount=47`; `native-slash-command-deploy-rest:get` to `/applications/1471498116237885474/commands`; `deploy-commands:done` ~4.8s; fingerprint persisted in plugin state:
      `sqlite3 … "SELECT entry_key, value_json FROM plugin_state_entries WHERE namespace='slash-command-deploy-hashes';"` → `1471498116237885474:default` with `global:reconcile` SHA256 `0adc06bf…9297aa9`.
    - **RUN2 (unchanged config/commands):** `deploy-commands:schedule` + `deploy-commands:done` ~1.6s; **zero** `native-slash-command-deploy-rest:*` lines (REST deploy skipped).

    Key log lines (RUN1):
```
deploy-commands:schedule … slashCommandDeploy=changed-only … commandCount=47
native-slash-command-deploy-rest:get:start … path=/applications/1471498116237885474/commands
native-slash-command-deploy-rest:get:done … requestMs=715
deploy-commands:done … ~4792ms
```

    Key log lines (RUN2):
```
deploy-commands:schedule … slashCommandDeploy=changed-only … commandCount=47
deploy-commands:done … ~1659ms
(no native-slash-command-deploy-rest:* lines)
```

  - Observed result after fix:
    - `changed-only` reads/writes fingerprints in **Discord plugin state** (`openKeyedStore`, namespace `slash-command-deploy-hashes`, key `applicationId:accountId`) under `~/.openclaw-discord-proof/state/openclaw.sqlite`.
    - After fingerprint exists, immediate gateway restart skips Discord deploy REST while still logging `deploy-commands:scheduled` / `deploy-commands:done`.
    - Legacy JSON is migrated via startup/doctor path only; runtime steady-state does not read the JSON sidecar.

  - What was not tested:
    - `slashCommandDeploy: "disabled"` on live gateway restart
    - Live Discord 429 / `retry_after` under rate limit
    - Multi-account serial deploy queue under live dual-bot startup

  - Before evidence (optional but encouraged):
    Prior behavior: every gateway restart unconditionally reconciled slash commands via Discord REST even when the command list was unchanged.



---

## Migration proof (commits b3282460 + 16ddd7f2)

**What was added after original live proof:**

Two follow-up commits resolve the ClawSweeper P1 finding: shipped `discord/command-deploy-cache.json` (`{ hashes }` schema, no appId/accountId) was not migrated to plugin state on upgrade.

**Fix:**
- `slash-command-deploy-state.ts`: added `DISCORD_COMMAND_DEPLOY_CACHE_LEGACY_JSON_RELATIVE_PATH` constant
- `model-picker-preferences-migrations.ts`: new migration plan reads `discord/command-deploy-cache.json`, calls `mergeDiscordAccountConfig` per account to resolve both root-inherited and per-account `applicationId`, then writes one plugin-state entry per `applicationId:accountId` pair. `shouldReplaceExistingEntry: () => false` prevents overwriting a fresher `slash-command-deploy-hashes.json` migration.

**Test run at head `7bf4b50` (macOS, Node v22.19.0, Vitest v4.1.7):**

```
pnpm test \
  extensions/discord/src/monitor/model-picker-preferences-migrations.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/monitor/slash-command-deploy-state.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/internal/client.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/monitor/commands.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/config-schema.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.startup.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.test.ts \
  -- --reporter=verbose

 Test Files  7 passed (7)
      Tests  127 passed (127)
   Start at  15:23:00
   Duration  3.17s
```

**Migration scenarios covered by new tests:**
- `command-deploy-cache.json` with root `applicationId` → single `appId:default` key
- `command-deploy-cache.json` with no `applicationId` → empty entries (graceful cold start)
- `command-deploy-cache.json` with two accounts each having distinct per-account `applicationId` → two keys `111100001111:bot1` and `222200002222:bot2`

**Doctor upgrade proof (current-head `7bf4b50`, Node v22.19.0, OpenClaw 2026.6.1):**

Simulated v2026.5.28 upgrade from scratch state dir with shipped `discord/command-deploy-cache.json`:

```
OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=~/.openclaw-proof-upgrade openclaw doctor --fix --yes --non-interactive
```

Doctor output:
```
[state-migrations] Auto-migrated legacy state:
- Migrated 1 Discord command deploy cache (v2026.5.28 and earlier) entry → plugin state
- Archived Discord command deploy cache (v2026.5.28 and earlier) legacy source →
  command-deploy-cache.json.migrated
```

SQLite verification after migration:
```
sqlite3 ... "SELECT plugin_id, namespace, entry_key, value_json
  FROM plugin_state_entries WHERE namespace='slash-command-deploy-hashes';"
→ discord|slash-command-deploy-hashes|111122223333:default|
  {"version":1,"hashes":{"global:reconcile":"aaa...a",
  "guild:1471499665584296039":"bbb...b"}}
```

Legacy file status:
```
$ ls discord/command-deploy-cache.json       # → No such file (migrated)
$ ls discord/command-deploy-cache.json.migrated  # → archived copy exists
```

**Combined proof:**
- Doctor --fix migrates v2026.5.28 shipped `command-deploy-cache.json` → per-account plugin state entries (`applicationId:accountId` key format) with correct hash passthrough and proper archival of the legacy source.
- Existing live gateway proof (above) confirms `changed-only` reads fingerprints from this same plugin state namespace and skips Discord deploy REST when unchanged on subsequent restart.

**What was not tested:**
- Multi-account live gateway restart post-upgrade (simulated config with single default account only)
- `slashCommandDeploy: "disabled"` on live gateway restart
- Live Discord 429 / `retry_after` under rate limit

target.title: Make typing indicator TTL configurable
target.body:
## Summary

- Increase the default typing indicator TTL from 2 minutes to 5 minutes.
- Add configurable `typingTtlSeconds` support via session config and agent defaults.
- Document the new setting and regenerate the base config schema.

Closes #71999

## Tests

- `pnpm config:schema:check`
- `node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/typing-persistence.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/reply-utils.test.ts src/config/schema.help.quality.test.ts`
- `pnpm tsgo:test:src`

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** `typingTtlSeconds` is accepted in both the real OpenClaw CLI config schema paths that this PR adds (`session.typingTtlSeconds` and `agents.defaults.typingTtlSeconds`).
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout on macOS, running the built CLI from this PR head `e4c78ec1c8bc` with an isolated temporary `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Created a temporary `openclaw.json` containing `session.typingTtlSeconds: 45` and `agents.defaults.typingTtlSeconds: 420`, then ran `pnpm openclaw --no-color config validate`, `pnpm openclaw --no-color config get session.typingTtlSeconds`, and `pnpm openclaw --no-color config get agents.defaults.typingTtlSeconds`.
- **Evidence after fix:** Terminal output from the real CLI:

  ```text
  > openclaw@2026.5.4 openclaw /Users/suntianpei/Documents/Playground/openclaw
  > node scripts/run-node.mjs --no-color config validate

  Config valid: /var/folders/.../openclaw.json

  > node scripts/run-node.mjs --no-color config get session.typingTtlSeconds
  45

  > node scripts/run-node.mjs --no-color config get agents.defaults.typingTtlSeconds
  420
  ```
- **Observed result after fix:** The real CLI validated the config successfully and read back both TTL values exactly, confirming the new config keys are accepted after the patch.
- **What was not tested:** No live chat-channel typing indicator was sent; this proof covers real CLI/config acceptance, with runtime TTL precedence covered by the focused automated tests.
